translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] the words which Moses hath spoken unto all Israel, beyond the Jordan, in the wilderness, in the plain over-against Suph, between Paran and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Di-Zahab;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: eleven days' from Horeb, the way of mount Seir, unto Kadesh-Barnea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first of the month hath Moses spoken unto the sons of Israel according to all that Jehovah hath commanded him concerning them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: after his smiting Sihon king of the Amorite who is dwelling in Heshbon, and Og king of Bashan who is dwelling in Ashtaroth in Edrei,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: beyond the Jordan, in the land of Moab, hath Moses begun to explain this law, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah our God hath spoken unto us in Horeb, saying, Enough to you -- of dwelling in this mount;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: turn ye and journey for you, and enter the mount of the Amorite, and unto all its neighbouring places, in the plain, in the hill-country, and in the low country, and in the south, and in the haven of the sea, the land of the Canaanite, and of Lebanon, unto the great river, the river Phrat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: see, I have set before you the land; go in and possess the land which Jehovah hath sworn to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them, and to their seed after them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I speak unto you at that time, saying, I am not able by myself to bear you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah your God hath multiplied you, and lo, ye [are] to-day as the stars of the heavens for multitude;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, God of your fathers, is adding to you, as ye [are], a thousand times, and doth bless you as He hath spoken to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'How do I bear by myself your pressure, and your burden, and your strife?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give for yourselves men, wise and intelligent, and known to your tribes, and I set them for your heads;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye answer me and say, Good [is] the thing which thou hast spoken -- to do.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I take the heads of your tribes, men, wise and known, and I appoint them heads over you, princes of thousands, and princes of hundreds, and princes of fifties, and princes of tens, and authorities, for your tribes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I command your judges at that time, saying, Hearkening between your brethren -- then ye have judged righteousness between a man, and his brother, and his sojourner;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye do not discern faces in judgment; as the little so the great ye do hear; ye are not afraid of the face of any, for the judgment is God's, and the thing which is too hard for you, ye bring near unto me, and I have heard it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I command you, at that time, all the things which ye do.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we journey from Horeb, and go [through] all that great and fearful wilderness which ye have seen -- the way of the hill-country of the Amorite, as Jehovah our God hath commanded us, and we come in unto Kadesh-Barnea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say unto you, Ye have come in unto the hill-country of the Amorite, which Jehovah our God is giving to us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: see, Jehovah thy God hath set before thee the land; go up, possess, as Jehovah, God of thy fathers, hath spoken to thee; fear not, nor be affrighted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye come near unto me, all of you, and say, Let us send men before us, and they search for us the land, and they bring us back word [concerning] the way in which we go up into it, and the cities unto which we come in;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the thing is good in mine eyes, and I take of you twelve men, one man for a tribe.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And they turn and go up to the hill-country, and come in unto the valley of Eshcol, and spy it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they take with their hand of the fruit of the land, and bring down unto us, and bring us back word, and say, Good is the land which Jehovah our God is giving to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye have not been willing to go up, and ye provoke the mouth of Jehovah your God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and murmur in your tents, and say, In Jehovah's hating us He hath brought us out of the land of Egypt, to give us into the hand of the Amorite -- to destroy us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whither are we going up? our brethren have melted our heart, saying, A people greater and taller than we, cities great and fenced to heaven, and also sons of Anakim -- we have seen there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say unto you, Be not terrified, nor be afraid of them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah your God, who is going before you -- He doth fight for you, according to all that He hath done with you in Egypt before your eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the wilderness, where thou hast seen that Jehovah thy God hath borne thee as a man beareth his son, in all the way which ye have gone, till your coming in unto this place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in this thing ye are not stedfast in Jehovah your God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who is going before you in the way to search out to you a place for your encamping, in fire by night, to shew you in the way in which ye go, and in a cloud by day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah heareth the voice of your words, and is wroth, and sweareth, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not one of these men of this evil generation doth see the good land which I have sworn to give to your fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: save Caleb son of Jephunneh -- he doth see it, and to him I give the land on which he hath trodden, and to his sons, because that he hath been fully after Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Also with me hath Jehovah been angry for your sake, saying, Also, thou dost not go in thither;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Joshua son of Nun, who is standing before thee, he goeth in thither; him strengthen thou; for he doth cause Israel to inherit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And your infants, of whom ye have said, For a prey they are, and your sons who have not known to-day good and evil, they go in thither, and to them I give it, and they possess it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye, turn for yourselves, and journey toward the wilderness, the way of the Red Sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye answer and say unto me, We have sinned against Jehovah; we -- we go up, and we have fought, according to all that which Jehovah our God hath commanded us; and ye gird on each his weapons of war, and ye are ready to go up into the hill-country;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah saith unto me, Say to them, Ye do not go up, nor fight, for I am not in your midst, and ye are not smitten before your enemies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I speak unto you, and ye have not hearkened, and provoke the mouth of Jehovah, and act proudly, and go up into the hill-country;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Amorite who is dwelling in that hill-country cometh out to meet you, and they pursue you as the bees do, and smite you in Seir -- unto Hormah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye turn back and weep before Jehovah, and Jehovah hath not hearkened to your voice, nor hath he given ear unto you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye dwell in Kadesh many days, according to the days which ye had dwelt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we turn, and journey into the wilderness, the way of the Red Sea, as Jehovah hath spoken unto me, and we go round the mount of Seir many days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah speaketh unto me, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Enough to you -- is the going round of this mount; turn for yourselves northward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the people command thou, saying, Ye are passing over into the border of your brethren, sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir, and they are afraid of you; and ye have been very watchful,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye do not strive with them, for I do not give to you of their land even the treading of the sole of a foot; for a possession to Esau I have given mount Seir.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Food ye buy from them with money, and have eaten; and also water ye buy from them with money, and have drunk,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee in all the work of thy hands; He hath known thy walking in this great wilderness these forty years; Jehovah thy God [is] with thee; thou hast not lacked anything.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we pass by from our brethren, sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir, by the way of the plain, by Elath, and by Ezion-Gaber; and we turn, and pass over the way of the wilderness of Moab;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah saith unto me, Do not distress Moab, nor stir thyself up against them [in] battle, for I do not give to thee of their land [for] a possession; for to the sons of Lot I have given Ar [for] a possession.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The Emim formerly have dwelt in it, a people great, and numerous, and tall, as the Anakim;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rephaim they are reckoned, they also, as the Anakim; and the Moabites call them Emim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in Seir have the Horim dwelt formerly; and the sons of Esau dispossess them, and destroy them from before them, and dwell in their stead, as Israel hath done to the land of his possession, which Jehovah hath given to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: now, rise ye, and pass over for yourselves the brook Zered; and we pass over the brook Zered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the days which we have walked from Kadesh-Barnea until that we have passed over the brook Zered, [are] thirty and eight years, till the consumption of all the generation of the men of battle from the midst of the camp, as Jehovah hath sworn to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and also the hand of Jehovah hath been against them, to destroy them from the midst of the camp, till they are consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it cometh to pass, when all the men of battle have finished dying from the midst of the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that Jehovah speaketh unto me, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou art passing over to-day the border of Moab, even Ar,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast come near over-against the sons of Ammon, thou dost not distress them, nor stir up thyself against them, for I do not give [any] of the land of the sons of Ammon to thee [for] a possession; for to the sons of Lot I have given it [for] a possession.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A land of Rephaim it is reckoned, even it; Rephaim dwelt in it formerly, and the Ammonites call them Zamzummim;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a people great, and numerous, and tall, as the Anakim, and Jehovah destroyeth them before them, and they dispossess them, and dwell in their stead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as He hath done for the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir, when He destroyed the Horim from before them, and they dispossess them, and dwell in their stead, unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'As to the Avim who are dwelling in Hazerim unto Azzah, the Caphtorim -- who are coming out from Caphtor -- have destroyed them, and dwell in their stead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Rise ye, journey and pass over the brook Arnon; see, I have given into thy hand Sihon king of Heshbon, the Amorite, and his land; begin to possess, and stir up thyself against him [in] battle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This day I begin to put thy dread and thy fear on the face of the peoples under the whole heavens, who hear thy fame, and have trembled and been pained because of thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I send messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth, unto Sihon king of Heshbon, -- words of peace -- saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let me pass over through thy land; in the several ways I go; I turn not aside -- right or left --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: food for money thou dost sell me, and I have eaten; and water for money thou dost give to me, and I have drunk; only, let me pass over on my feet, --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as the sons of Esau who are dwelling in Seir, and the Moabites who are dwelling in Ar, have done to me -- till that I pass over the Jordan, unto the land which Jehovah our God is giving to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Sihon king of Heshbon hath not been willing to let us pass over by him, for Jehovah thy God hath hardened his spirit, and strengthened his heart, so as to give him into thy hand as at this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah saith unto me, See, I have begun to give before thee Sihon and his land; begin to possess -- to possess his land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Sihon cometh out to meet us, he and all his people, to battle to Jahaz;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah our God giveth him before us, and we smite him, and his sons, and all his people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we capture all his cities at that time, and devote the whole city, men, and the women, and the infants -- we have not left a remnant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only, the cattle we have spoiled for ourselves, and the spoil of the cities which we have captured.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'From Aroer, which [is] by the edge of the brook Arnon, and the city which [is] by the brook, even unto Gilead there hath not been a city which [is] too high for us; the whole hath Jehovah our God given before us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, unto the land of the sons of Ammon thou hast not drawn near, any part of the brook Jabbok, and cities of the hill-country, and anything which Jehovah our God hath [not] commanded.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we turn, and go up the way to Bashan, and Og king of Bashan cometh out to meet us, he and all his people, to battle, [to] Edrei.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah saith unto me, Fear him not, for into thy hand I have given him, and all his people, and his land, and thou hast done to him as thou hast done to Sihon king of the Amorite who is dwelling in Heshbon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah our God giveth into our hands also Og king of Bashan, and all his people, and we smite him till there hath not been left to him a remnant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we capture all his cities at that time, there hath not been a city which we have not taken from them, sixty cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All these [are] cities fenced with high walls, two-leaved doors and bar, apart from cities of villages very many;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we devote them, as we have done to Sihon king of Heshbon, devoting every city, men, the women, and the infants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the cattle, and the spoil of the cities, we have spoiled for ourselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we take, at that time, the land out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorite, which is beyond the Jordan, from the brook Arnon unto mount Hermon;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (Sidonians call Hermon, Sirion; and the Amorites call it Senir,)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all the cities of the plain, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, unto Salchah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for only Og king of Bashan had been left of the remnant of the Rephaim; lo, his bedstead [is] a bedstead of iron; is it not in Rabbath of the sons of Ammon? nine cubits its length, and four cubits its breadth, by the cubit of a man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this land we have possessed, at that time; from Aroer, which [is] by the brook Arnon, and the half of mount Gilead, and its cities, I have given to the Reubenite, and to the Gadite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the rest of Gilead and all Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I have given to the half tribe of Manasseh; all the region of Argob, to all that Bashan, called the land of Rephaim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jair son of Manasseh hath taken all the region of Argob, unto the border of Geshuri, and Maachathi, and calleth them by his own name, Bashan-Havoth-Jair, unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Machir I have given Gilead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And to the Reubenite and to the Gadite I have given from Gilead even unto the brook Arnon, the middle of the valley and the border, even unto Jabbok the brook, the border of the sons of Ammon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the plain, and the Jordan, and the border, from Chinnereth even unto the sea of the plain, the salt sea, under the springs of Pisgah, at the [sun] -rising.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I command you, at that time, saying, Jehovah your God hath given to you this land to possess it; armed ye pass over before your brethren the sons of Israel, all the sons of might.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, your wives, and your infants, and your cattle -- I have known that ye have much cattle -- do dwell in your cities which I have given to you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: till that Jehovah give rest to your brethren like yourselves, and they also have possessed the land which Jehovah your God is giving to them beyond the Jordan, then ye have turned back each to his possession, which I have given to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehoshua I have commanded at that time, saying, Thine eyes are seeing all that which Jehovah your God hath done to these two kings -- so doth Jehovah to all the kingdoms whither thou are passing over;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: fear them not, for Jehovah your God, He is fighting for you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I entreat for grace unto Jehovah, at that time, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lord Jehovah, Thou -- Thou hast begun to shew Thy servant Thy greatness, and Thy strong hand; for who [is] a God in the heavens or in earth who doth according to Thy works, and according to Thy might?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let me pass over, I pray Thee, and see the good land which [is] beyond the Jordan, this good hill-country, and Lebanon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah sheweth himself wroth with me, for your sake, and hath not hearkened unto me, and Jehovah saith unto me, Enough for thee; add not to speak unto Me any more about this thing:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: go up [to] the top of Pisgah, and lift up thine eyes westward, and northward, and southward, and eastward, and see with thine eyes -- for thou dost not pass over this Jordan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and charge Jehoshua, and strengthen him, and harden him, for he doth pass over before this people, and he doth cause them to inherit the land which thou seest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we dwell in a valley over-against Beth-Peor.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, Israel, hearken unto the statutes, and unto the judgments which I am teaching you to do, so that ye live, and have gone in, and possessed the land which Jehovah God of your fathers is giving to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ye do not add to the word which I am commanding you, nor diminish from it, to keep the commands of Jehovah your God which I am commanding you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Your eyes are seeing that which Jehovah hath done in Baal-Peor, for every man who hath gone after Baal-Peor, Jehovah thy God hath destroyed him from thy midst;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye who are cleaving to Jehovah your God, [are] alive, all of you, to-day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'See, I have taught you statutes and judgments, as Jehovah my God hath commanded me -- to do so, in the midst of the land whither ye are going in to possess it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have kept and done [them] (for it [is] your wisdom and your understanding) before the eyes of the peoples who hear all these statutes, and they have said, Only, a people wise and understanding [is] this great nation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For which [is] the great nation that hath God near unto it, as Jehovah our God, in all we have called unto him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and which [is] the great nation which hath righteous statutes and judgments according to all this law which I am setting before you to-day?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, take heed to thyself, and watch thy soul exceedingly, lest thou forget the things which thine eyes have seen, and lest they turn aside from thy heart, all days of thy life; and thou hast made them known to thy sons, and to thy sons' sons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The day when thou hast stood before Jehovah thy God in Horeb -- in Jehovah's saying unto me, Assemble to Me the people, and I cause them to hear My words, so that they learn to fear Me all the days that they are alive on the ground, and their sons they teach; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye draw near and stand under the mountain, and the mountain is burning with fire unto the heart of the heavens -- darkness, cloud, yea, thick darkness:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah speaketh unto you out of the midst of the fire; a voice of words ye are hearing and a similitude ye are not seeing, only a voice;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He declareth to you His covenant, which He hath commanded you to do, the Ten Matters, and He writeth them upon two tables of stone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And me hath Jehovah commanded at that time to teach you statutes and judgments, for your doing them in the land whither ye are passing over to possess it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have been very watchful of your souls, for ye have not seen any similitude in the day of Jehovah's speaking unto you in Horeb out of the midst of the fire,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest ye do corruptly, and have made to you a graven image, a similitude of any figure, a form of male or female --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a form of any beast which [is] in the earth -- a form of any winged bird which flieth in the heavens --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a form of any creeping thing on the ground -- a form of any fish which [is] in the waters under the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And lest thou lift up thine eyes towards the heavens, and hast seen the sun, and the moon, and the stars, all the host of the heavens, and thou hast been forced, and hast bowed thyself to them, and served them, which Jehovah thy God hath apportioned to all the peoples under the whole heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And you hath Jehovah taken, and He is bringing you out from the iron furnace, from Egypt, to be to Him for a people -- an inheritance, as [at] this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah hath shewed himself wroth with me because of your words, and sweareth to my not passing over the Jordan, and to my not going in unto the good land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I am dying in this land; I am not passing over the Jordan, and ye are passing over, and have possessed this good land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to yourselves, lest ye forget the covenant of Jehovah your God, which He hath made with you, and have made to yourselves a graven image, a similitude of anything [concerning] which Jehovah thy God hath charged thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah thy God is a fire consuming -- a zealous God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou begettest sons and sons' sons, and ye have become old in the land, and have done corruptly, and have made a graven image, a similitude of anything, and have done the evil thing in the eyes of Jehovah, to provoke Him to anger: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have caused to testify against you this day the heavens and the earth, that ye do perish utterly hastily from off the land whither ye are passing over the Jordan to possess it; ye do not prolong days upon it, but are utterly destroyed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath scattered you among the peoples, and ye have been left few in number among the nations, whither Jehovah leadeth you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have served there gods, work of man's hands, wood and stone, which see not, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And -- ye have sought from thence Jehovah thy God, and hast found, when thou seekest Him with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in distress [being] to thee, and all these things have found thee, in the latter end of the days, and thou hast turned back unto Jehovah thy God, and hast hearkened to His voice;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a merciful God [is] Jehovah thy God; He doth not fail thee, nor destroy thee, nor forget the covenant of thy fathers, which He hath sworn to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For, ask, I pray thee, at the former days which have been before thee, from the day that God prepared man on the earth, and from the [one] end of the heavens even unto the [other] end of the heavens, whether there hath been as this great thing -- or hath been heard like it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath a people heard the voice of God speaking out of the midst of the fire, as thou hast heard, thou -- and doth live?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Or hath God tried to go in to take to Himself, a nation from the midst of a nation, by trials, by signs, and by wonders, and by war, and by a strong hand, and by a stretched-out arm, and by great terrors -- according to all that Jehovah your God hath done to you, in Egypt, before your eyes?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, thou hast been shewn [it], to know that Jehovah He [is] God; there is none else besides Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'From the heavens He hath caused thee to hear His voice, to instruct thee, and on earth He hath shewed thee His great fire, and His words thou hast heard out of the midst of the fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And because that He hath loved thy fathers, He doth also fix on their seed after them, and doth bring thee out, in His presence, by His great power, from Egypt:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to dispossess nations greater and stronger than thou, from thy presence, to bring thee in to give to thee their land -- an inheritance, as [at] this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast known to-day, and hast turned [it] back unto thy heart, that Jehovah He [is] God, in the heavens above, and on the earth beneath -- there is none else;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast kept His statutes and His commands which I am commanding thee to-day, so that it is well to thee, and to thy sons after thee, and so that thou prolongest days on the ground which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- all the days.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Moses separateth three cities beyond the Jordan, towards the sun-rising,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the fleeing thither of the man-slayer, who slayeth his neighbour unknowingly, and he is not hating him heretofore, and he hath fled unto one of these cities, and he hath lived:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bezer, in the wilderness, in the land of the plain, of the Reubenite; and Ramoth, in Gilead, of the Gadite; and Golan, in Bashan, of the Manassahite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this [is] the law which Moses hath set before the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these [are] the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which Moses hath spoken unto the sons of Israel, in their coming out of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: beyond the Jordan, in the valley over-against Beth-Peor, in the land of Sihon, king of the Amorite, who is dwelling in Heshbon, whom Moses and the sons of Israel have smitten, in their coming out of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they possess his land, and the land of Og king of Bashan, two kings of the Amorite who [are] beyond the Jordan, [towards] the sun-rising;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from Aroer, which [is] by the edge of the brook Arnon, even unto mount Sion, which [is] Hermon --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the plain beyond the Jordan eastward, even unto the sea of the plain, under the springs of Pisgah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses calleth unto all Israel, and saith unto them, 'Hear, Israel, the statutes and the judgments which I am speaking in your ears to-day, and ye have learned them, and have observed to do them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah our God made with us a covenant in Horeb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not with our fathers hath Jehovah made this covenant, but with us; we -- these -- here to-day -- all of us alive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Face to face hath Jehovah spoken with you, in the mount, out of the midst of the fire;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am standing between Jehovah and you, at that time, to declare to you the word of Jehovah, for ye have been afraid from the presence of the fire, and ye have not gone up into the mount; saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I Jehovah [am] thy God, who hath brought thee out from the land of Egypt, from a house of servants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou hast no other gods in My presence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not make to thee a graven image, any similitude which [is] in the heavens above, and which [is] in the earth beneath, and which [is] in the waters under the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not bow thyself to them nor serve them, for I Jehovah thy God [am] a zealous God, charging iniquity of fathers on children, and on a third [generation], and on a fourth, to those hating Me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and doing kindness to thousands, to those loving Me, and to those keeping My commands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not take up the Name of Jehovah thy God for a vain thing, for Jehovah doth not acquit him who taketh up His Name for a vain thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Observe the day of the sabbath -- to sanctify it, as Jehovah thy God hath commanded thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: six days thou dost labour, and hast done all thy work,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the seventh day [is] a sabbath to Jehovah thy God; thou dost not do any work, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy handmaid, and thine ox, and thine ass, and all thy cattle, and thy sojourner who [is] within thy gates; so that thy man-servant, and thy handmaid doth rest like thyself;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered that a servant thou hast been in the land of Egypt, and Jehovah thy God is bringing thee out thence by a strong hand, and by a stretched-out arm; therefore hath Jehovah thy God commanded thee to keep the day of the sabbath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Honour thy father and thy mother, as Jehovah thy God hath commanded thee, so that thy days are prolonged, and so that it is well with thee, on the ground which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not murder.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not commit adultery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not steal.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not answer against thy neighbour -- a false testimony.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not desire thy neighbour's wife; nor dost thou covet thy neighbour's house, his field, and his man-servant, and his handmaid, his ox, and his ass, and anything which [is] thy neighbour's.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These words hath Jehovah spoken unto all your assembly, in the mount out of the midst of the fire, of the cloud, and of the thick darkness -- a great voice; and He hath not added, and He writeth them on two tables of stone, and giveth them unto me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it cometh to pass as ye hear the voice out of the midst of the darkness, and of the mountain burning with fire, that ye come near unto me, all the heads of your tribes, and your elders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and say, Lo, Jehovah our God hath shewed us His honour, and His greatness; and His voice we have heard out of the midst of the fire; this day we have seen that God doth speak with man -- and he hath lived.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, now, why do we die? for consume us doth this great fire -- if we add to hear the voice of Jehovah our God any more -- then we have died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For who of all flesh [is] he who hath heard the voice of the living God speaking out of the midst of the fire like us -- and doth live?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Draw near thou, and hear all that which Jehovah our God saith, and thou, thou dost speak unto us all that which Jehovah our God speaketh unto thee, and we have hearkened, and done it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah heareth the voice of your words, in your speaking unto me, and Jehovah saith unto me, I have heard the voice of the words of this people which they have spoken unto thee; they have done well [in] all that they have spoken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that their heart had been thus to them, to fear Me, and to keep My commands all the days, that it may be well with them, and with their sons -- to the age!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Go, say to them, Turn back for yourselves, to your tents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou here stand thou with Me, and let Me speak unto thee all the command, and the statutes, and the judgments which thou dost teach them, and they have done in the land which I am giving to them to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye have observed to do as Jehovah your God hath commanded you, ye turn not aside -- right or left;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in all the way which Jehovah your God hath commanded you ye walk, so that ye live, and [it is] well with you, and ye have prolonged days in the land which ye possess.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this [is] the command, the statutes and the judgments which Jehovah your God hath commanded to teach you, to do in the land which ye are passing over thither to possess it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that thou dost fear Jehovah thy God, to keep all His statutes and His commands, which I am commanding thee, thou, and thy son, and thy son's son, all days of thy life, and so that thy days are prolonged.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast heard, O Israel, and observed to do, that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest multiply exceedingly, as Jehovah, God of thy fathers, hath spoken to thee, [in] the land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hear, O Israel, Jehovah our God [is] one Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast loved Jehovah thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these words which I am commanding thee to-day have been on thine heart,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast repeated them to thy sons, and spoken of them in thy sitting in thine house, and in thy walking in the way, and in thy lying down, and in thy rising up,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast bound them for a sign upon thy hand, and they have been for frontlets between thine eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast written them on door-posts of thy house, and on thy gates.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when Jehovah thy God doth bring thee in unto the land which He hath sworn to thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to thee -- cities great and good, which thou hast not built,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and houses full of all good things which thou hast not filled, and wells digged which thou hast not digged, vineyards and olive-yards which thou hast not planted, and thou hast eaten, and been satisfied;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to thyself lest thou forget Jehovah who hath brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of a house of servants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah thy God thou dost fear, and Him thou dost serve, and by His name thou dost swear;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye do not go after other gods, of the gods of the peoples who [are] round about you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a zealous God [is] Jehovah thy God in thy midst -- lest the anger of Jehovah thy God burn against thee, and He hath destroyed thee from off the face of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye do not try Jehovah your God as ye tried in Massah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye do diligently keep the commands of Jehovah your God, and His testimonies, and His statutes which He hath commanded thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast done that which is right and good in the eyes of Jehovah, so that it is well with thee, and thou hast gone in and possessed the good land which Jehovah hath sworn to thy fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to drive away all thine enemies from thy presence, as Jehovah hath spoken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thy son asketh thee hereafter, saying, What [are] the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which Jehovah our God hath commanded you?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast said to thy son, Servants we have been to Pharaoh in Egypt, and Jehovah bringeth us out of Egypt by a high hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah giveth signs and wonders, great and sad, on Egypt, on Pharaoh, and on all his house, before our eyes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and us He hath brought out thence, in order to bring us in, to give to us the land which He had sworn to our fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah commandeth us to do all these statutes, to fear Jehovah our God, for good to ourselves all the days, to keep us alive, as [at] this day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and righteousness it is for us, when we observe to do all this command before Jehovah our God, as He hath commanded us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When Jehovah thy God doth bring thee in unto the land whither thou art going in to possess it, and He hath cast out many nations from thy presence, the Hittite, and the Girgashite, and the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, seven nations more numerous and mighty than thou,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath given them before thee, and thou hast smitten them -- thou dost utterly devote them -- thou dost not make with them a covenant, nor dost thou favour them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou dost not join in marriage with them; thy daughter thou dost not give to his son, and his daughter thou dost not take to thy son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he doth turn aside thy son from after Me, and they have served other gods, and the anger of Jehovah hath burned against you, and hath destroyed thee hastily.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But thus thou dost to them: their altars ye break down, and their standing pillars ye shiver, and their shrines ye cut down, and their graven images ye burn with fire;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a holy people [art] thou to Jehovah thy God; on thee hath Jehovah thy God fixed, to be to Him for a peculiar people, out of all the peoples who [are] on the face of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Not because of your being more numerous than any of the peoples hath Jehovah delighted in you, and fixeth on you, for ye [are] the least of all the peoples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but because of Jehovah's loving you, and because of His keeping the oath which He hath sworn to your fathers, hath Jehovah brought you out by a strong hand, and doth ransom you from a house of servants, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast known that Jehovah thy God He [is] God, the faithful God, keeping the covenant, and the kindness, to those loving Him, and to those keeping His commands -- to a thousand generations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and repaying to those hating Him, unto their face, to destroy them; He delayeth not to him who is hating Him -- unto his face, He repayeth to him --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast kept the command, and the statutes, and the judgments, which I am commanding thee to-day to do them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, because ye hear these judgments, and have kept, and done them, that Jehovah thy God hath kept to thee the covenant and the kindness which He hath sworn to thy fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hath loved thee, and blessed thee, and multiplied thee, and hath blessed the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, thy corn, and thy new wine, and thine oil, the increase of thine oxen, and the wealth of thy flock, on the ground which He hath sworn to thy fathers to give to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed art thou above all the peoples, there is not in thee a barren man or a barren woman -- nor among your cattle;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath turned aside from thee every sickness, and none of the evil diseases of Egypt (which thou hast known) doth He put on thee, and He hath put them on all hating thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast consumed all the peoples whom Jehovah thy God is giving to thee; thine eye hath no pity on them, and thou dost not serve their gods, for a snare it [is] to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou sayest in thine heart, These nations [are] more numerous than I, how am I able to dispossess them? --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou art not afraid of them; thou dost surely remember that which Jehovah thy God hath done to Pharaoh, and to all Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the great trials which thine eyes have seen, and the signs, and the wonders, and the strong hand, and the stretched-out arm, with which Jehovah thy God hath brought thee out; so doth Jehovah thy God to all the peoples of whose presence thou art afraid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And also the locust doth Jehovah thy God send among them, till the destruction of those who are left, and of those who are hidden from thy presence;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou art not terrified by their presence, for Jehovah thy God [is] in thy midst, a God great and fearful.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah thy God hath cast out these nations from thy presence little [by] little, (thou art not able to consume them hastily, lest the beast of the field multiply against thee),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath given them before thee, and destroyed them -- a great destruction -- till their destruction;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He hath given their kings into thy hand, and thou hast destroyed their name from under the heavens; no man doth station himself in thy presence till thou hast destroyed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The graven images of their gods ye do burn with fire; thou dost not desire the silver and gold on them, nor hast thou taken [it] to thyself, lest thou be snared by it, for the abomination of Jehovah thy God it [is];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou dost not bring in an abomination unto thy house -- or thou hast been devoted like it; -- thou dost utterly detest it, and thou dost utterly abominate it; for it [is] devoted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'All the command which I am commanding thee to-day ye observe to do, so that ye live, and have multiplied, and gone in, and possessed the land which Jehovah hath sworn to your fathers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered all the way which Jehovah thy God hath caused thee to go these forty years in the wilderness, in order to humble thee to try thee, to know that which [is] in thy heart, whether thou dost keep His commands or not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And He doth humble thee, and cause thee to hunger and doth cause thee to eat the manna (which thou hast not known, even thy fathers have not known), in order to cause thee to know that not by bread alone doth man live, but by every produce of the mouth of Jehovah man doth live.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thy raiment hath not worn out from off thee, and thy foot hath not swelled these forty years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast known, with thy heart, that as a man chastiseth his son Jehovah thy God is chastising thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast kept the commands of Jehovah thy God, to walk in His ways, and to fear Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For Jehovah thy God is bringing thee in unto a good land, a land of brooks of waters, of fountains, and of depths coming out in valley and in mountain:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a land of wheat, and barley, and vine, and fig, and pomegranate; a land of oil olive and honey;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a land in which without scarcity thou dost eat bread, thou dost not lack anything in it; a land whose stones [are] iron, and out of its mountains thou dost dig brass;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast eaten, and been satisfied, and hast blessed Jehovah thy God, on the good land which he hath given to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to thyself, lest thou forget Jehovah thy God so as not to keep His commands, and His judgments, and His statutes which I am commanding thee to-day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest thou eat, and hast been satisfied, and good houses dost build, and hast inhabited;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy herd and thy flock be multiplied, and silver and gold be multiplied to thee; and all that is thine be multiplied:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thy heart hath been high, and thou hast forgotten Jehovah thy God (who is bringing thee out of the land of Egypt, out of a house of servants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who is causing thee to go in the great and the terrible wilderness -- burning serpent, and scorpion, and thirst -- where there is no water; who is bringing out to thee waters from the flinty rock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who is causing thee to eat manna in the wilderness, which thy fathers have not known, in order to humble thee, and in order to try thee, to do thee good in thy latter end),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast said in thy heart, My power, and the might of my hand, hath made for me this wealth:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast remembered Jehovah thy God, for He it [is] who is giving to thee power to make wealth, in order to establish His covenant which He hath sworn to thy fathers as [at] this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been -- if thou really forget Jehovah thy God, and hast gone after other gods, and served them, and bowed thyself to them, I have testified against you to-day that ye do utterly perish;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as the nations whom Jehovah is destroying from your presence, so ye perish; because ye hearken not to the voice of Jehovah your God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hear, Israel, thou art passing over to-day the Jordan, to go in to possess nations greater and mightier than thyself; cities great and fenced in the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a people great and tall, sons of Anakim, whom thou -- thou hast known, (and thou -- thou hast heard: Who doth station himself before sons of Anak?)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast known to-day, that Jehovah thy God [is] He who is passing over before thee -- a fire consuming; He doth destroy them, and He doth humble them before thee, and thou hast dispossessed them, and destroyed them hastily, as Jehovah hath spoken to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not speak in thy heart (in Jehovah thy God's driving them away from before thee), saying, For my righteousness hath Jehovah brought me in to possess this land, seeing for the wickedness of these nations is Jehovah dispossessing them from thy presence;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not for thy righteousness, and for the uprightness of thy heart, art thou going in to possess their land; but for the wickedness of these nations is Jehovah thy God dispossessing them from before thee; and in order to establish the word which Jehovah hath sworn to thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast known, that not for thy righteousness is Jehovah thy God giving to thee this good land to possess it, for a people stiff of neck thou [art].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Remember -- do not forget -- that [with] which thou hast made Jehovah thy God wroth in the wilderness; even from the day that thou hast come out of the land of Egypt till your coming in unto this place rebels ye have been with Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: even in Horeb ye have made Jehovah wroth, and Jehovah sheweth Himself angry against you -- to destroy you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'In my going up into the mount to receive the tables of stone (tables of the covenant which Jehovah hath made with you), and I abide in the mount forty days and forty nights; bread I have not eaten, and water I have not drunk;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah giveth unto me the two tables of stone written with the finger of God, and on them according to all the words which Jehovah hath spoken with you in the mount, out of the midst of the fire, in the day of the assembly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it cometh to pass, at the end of forty days and forty nights, Jehovah hath given unto me the two tables of stone -- tables of the covenant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah saith unto me, Rise, go down, hasten from this, for thy people hath done corruptly, whom thou hast brought out of Egypt; they have turned aside hastily out of the way which I have commanded them -- they have made to themselves a molten thing!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah speaketh unto me, saying, I have seen this people, and lo, a people stiff of neck it [is];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: desist from Me, and I destroy them, and blot out their name from under the heavens, and I make thee become a nation more mighty and numerous than it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I turn, and come down from the mount, and the mount is burning with fire, and the two tables of the covenant on my two hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I see, and lo, ye have sinned against Jehovah your God; ye have made to yourselves a molten calf; ye have turned aside hastily out of the way which Jehovah hath commanded you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I lay hold on the two tables, and cast them out of my two hands, and break them before your eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I throw myself before Jehovah, as at first, forty days and forty nights; bread I have not eaten, and water I have not drunk, because of all your sins which ye have sinned, by doing the evil thing in the eyes of Jehovah, to make Him angry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For I have been afraid because of the anger and the fury with which Jehovah hath been wroth against you, to destroy you; and Jehovah doth hearken unto me also at this time.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And with Aaron hath Jehovah shewed himself very angry, to destroy him, and I pray also for Aaron at that time;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your sin, which ye have made -- the calf -- I have taken, and I burn it with fire, and beat it, grinding well till that it [is] small as dust, and I cast its dust unto the brook which is going down out of the mount.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in Taberah, and in Massah, and in Kibroth-Hattaavah, ye have been making Jehovah wroth:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in Jehovah's sending you from Kadesh-Barnea, saying, Go up, and possess the land which I have given to you, then ye provoke the mouth of Jehovah your God, and have not given credence to Him, nor hearkened to His voice;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rebels ye have been with Jehovah from the day of my knowing you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I throw myself before Jehovah, the forty days and the forty nights, as I had thrown myself, for Jehovah hath said -- to destroy you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I pray unto Jehovah, and say, Lord Jehovah, destroy not Thy people, and Thine inheritance, whom Thou hast ransomed in Thy greatness; whom Thou hast brought out of Egypt with a strong hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be mindful of Thy servants, of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, turn not unto the stiffness of this people, and unto its wickedness, and unto its sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest the land say from which Thou hast brought us out, Because of Jehovah's want of ability to bring them in unto the land of which He hath spoken to them, and because of His hating them, He brought them out to put them to death in the wilderness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they [are] Thy people, and Thine inheritance, whom Thou hast brought out by Thy great power, and by Thy stretched-out arm!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'At that time hath Jehovah said unto me, Grave for thee two tables of stone, like the first, and come up unto Me, into the mount, and thou hast made for thee an ark of wood,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I write on the tables the words which were on the first tables, which thou hast broken, and thou hast placed them in the ark;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I make an ark of shittim wood, and grave two tables of stone like the first, and go up to the mount, and the two tables in my hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And He writeth on the tables, according to the first writing, the Ten Matters, which Jehovah hath spoken unto you in the mount, out of the midst of the fire, in the day of the assembly, and Jehovah giveth them unto me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I turn and come down from the mount, and put the tables in the ark which I had made, and they are there, as Jehovah commanded me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the sons of Israel have journeyed from Beeroth of the sons of Jaakan to Mosera, there Aaron died, and he is buried there, and Eleazar his son doth act as priest in his stead;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thence they journeyed to Gudgodah, and from Gudgodah to Jotbathah, a land of brooks of water.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'At that time hath Jehovah separated the tribe of Levi, to bear the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, to stand before Jehovah, to serve Him, and to bless in His name, unto this day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore there hath not been to Levi a portion and inheritance with his brethren; Jehovah Himself [is] his inheritance, as Jehovah thy God hath spoken to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I -- I have stood in the mount, as the former days, forty days and forty nights, and Jehovah hearkeneth unto me also at that time; Jehovah hath not willed to destroy thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah saith unto me, Rise, go to journey before the people, and they go in and possess the land which I have sworn to their fathers to give to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, Israel, what is Jehovah thy God asking from thee, except to fear Jehovah thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him, and to serve Jehovah thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to keep the commands of Jehovah, and His statutes which I am commanding thee to-day, for good to thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, to Jehovah thy God [are] the heavens and the heavens of the heavens, the earth and all that [is] in it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only in thy fathers hath Jehovah delighted -- to love them, and He doth fix on their seed after them -- on you, out of all the peoples as [at] this day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have circumcised the foreskin of your heart, and your neck ye do not harden any more;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah your God -- He [is] God of the gods, and Lord of the lords; God, the great, the mighty, and the fearful; who accepteth not persons, nor taketh a bribe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is doing the judgment of fatherless and widow, and loving the sojourner, to give to him bread and raiment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye have loved the sojourner, for sojourners ye were in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah thy God thou dost fear, Him thou dost serve, and to Him thou dost cleave, and by His name thou dost swear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He [is] thy praise, and He [is] thy God, who hath done with thee these great and fearful [things] which thine eyes have seen:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with seventy persons did thy fathers go down to Egypt, and now hath Jehovah thy God made thee as stars of the heavens for multitude.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast loved Jehovah thy God, and kept His charge, and His statutes, and His judgments, and His commands, all the days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have known to-day -- for it is not your sons who have not known, and who have not seen the chastisement of Jehovah your God, His greatness, His strong hand, and His stretched-out arm,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and His signs, and His doings, which He hath done in the midst of Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt, and to all his land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that which He hath done to the force of Egypt, to its horses, and to its chariot, when He hath caused the waters of the Red Sea to flow against their faces in their pursuing after them, and Jehovah destroyeth them, unto this day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that which He hath done to you in the wilderness, till your coming in unto this place;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that which He hath done to Dathan, and to Abiram, sons of Eliab, sons of Reuben, when the earth hath opened her mouth and swalloweth them, and their houses, and their tents, and all that liveth, which is at their feet, in the midst of all Israel:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ' -- But [it is] your eyes which are seeing all the great work of Jehovah, which He hath done;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have kept all the command which I am commanding thee to-day, so that ye are strong, and have gone in, and possessed the land whither ye are passing over to possess it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and so that ye prolong days on the ground which Jehovah hath sworn to your fathers to give to them and to their seed -- a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For the land whither thou art going in to possess it, is not as the land of Egypt whence ye have come out, where thou sowest thy seed, and hast watered with thy foot, as a garden of the green herb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the land whither ye are passing over to possess it, [is] a land of hills and valleys; of the rain of the heavens it drinketh water;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a land which Jehovah thy God is searching; continually [are] the eyes of Jehovah thy God upon it, from the beginning of the year even unto the latter end of the year.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been -- if thou hearken diligently unto My commands which I am commanding you to-day, to love Jehovah your God, and to serve Him with all your heart, and with all your soul --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that I have given the rain of your land in its season -- sprinkling and gathered -- and thou hast gathered thy corn, and thy new wine, and thine oil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have given herbs in thy field for thy cattle, and thou hast eaten, and been satisfied.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to yourselves, lest your heart be enticed, and ye have turned aside, and served other gods, and bowed yourselves to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anger of Jehovah hath burned against you, and He hath restrained the heavens, and there is no rain, and the ground doth not give her increase, and ye have perished hastily from off the good land which Jehovah is giving to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye have placed these my words on your heart, and on your soul, and have bound them for a sign on your hand, and they have been for frontlets between your eyes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have taught them to your sons, by speaking of them in thy sitting in thy house, and in thy going in the way, and in thy lying down, and in thy rising up,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast written them on the side-posts of thy house, and on thy gates,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that your days are multiplied, and the days of your sons, on the ground which Jehovah hath sworn to your fathers to give to them, as the days of the heavens on the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For, if ye diligently keep all this command which I am commanding you -- to do it, to love Jehovah your God, to walk in all His ways, and to cleave to Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then hath Jehovah dispossessed all these nations from before you, and ye have possessed nations, greater and mightier than you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every place on which the sole of your foot treadeth is yours; from the wilderness, and Lebanon, from the river, the river Phrat, even unto the farther sea is your border;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: no man doth station himself in your presence; your dread and your fear doth Jehovah your God put on the face of all the land on which ye tread, as He hath spoken to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'See, I am setting before you to-day a blessing and a reviling:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the blessing, when ye hearken unto the commands of Jehovah your God, which I am commanding you to-day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the reviling, if ye do not hearken unto the commands of Jehovah your God, and have turned aside out of the way which I am commanding you to-day, to go after other gods which ye have not known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when Jehovah thy God doth bring thee in unto the land whither thou art going in to possess it, that thou hast given the blessing on mount Gerizim, and the reviling on mount Ebal;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: are they not beyond the Jordan, behind the way of the going in of the sun, in the land of the Canaanite, who is dwelling in the plain over-against Gilgal, near the oaks of Moreh?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for ye are passing over the Jordan to go in to possess the land which Jehovah your God is giving to you; and ye have possessed it, and dwelt in it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and observed to do all the statutes and the judgments which I am setting before you to day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These [are] the statutes and the judgments which ye observe to do in the land which Jehovah, God of thy fathers, hath given to thee to possess it, all the days that ye are living on the ground:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye do utterly destroy all the places where the nations which ye are dispossessing served their gods, on the high mountains, and on the heights, and under every green tree;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have broken down their altars, and shivered their standing pillars, and their shrines ye burn with fire, and graven images of their gods ye cut down, and have destroyed their name out of that place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye do not do so to Jehovah your God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but unto the place which Jehovah your God doth choose out of all your tribes to put His name there, to His tabernacle ye seek, and thou hast entered thither,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast brought in thither your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, and your tithes, and the heave-offering of your hand, and your vows, and your free-will offerings, and the firstlings of your herd and of your flock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have eaten there before Jehovah your God, and have rejoiced in every putting forth of your hand, ye and your households, with which Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye do not do according to all that we are doing here to-day, each anything that is right in his own eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for ye have not come in hitherto unto the rest, and unto the inheritance, which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have passed over the Jordan, and have dwelt in the land which Jehovah your God is causing you to inherit, and He hath given rest to you from all your enemies round about, and ye have dwelt confidently:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, the place on which Jehovah your God doth fix to cause His name to tabernacle there, thither ye bring in all that which I am commanding you, your burnt-offerings, and your sacrifices, your tithes, and the heave-offering of your hand, and all the choice of your vows which ye vow to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have rejoiced before Jehovah your God, ye, and your sons, and your daughters, and your men-servants, and your handmaids, and the Levite who [is] within your gates, for he hath no part and inheritance with you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to thee, lest thou cause thy burnt-offerings to ascend in any place which thou seest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: except in the place which Jehovah doth choose in one of thy tribes, there thou dost cause thy burnt-offerings to ascend, and there thou dost do all that which I am commanding thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, with all the desire of thy soul thou dost sacrifice, and hast eaten flesh according to the blessing of Jehovah thy God which He hath given to thee, in all thy gates; the unclean and the clean do eat it, as of the roe, and as of the hart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, the blood ye do not eat -- on the earth thou dost pour it as water;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
