translations.text: thou art not able to eat within thy gates the tithe of thy corn, and of thy new wine, and thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herd and of thy flock, and any of thy vows which thou vowest, and thy free-will offerings, and heave-offering of thy hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but before Jehovah thy God thou dost eat it, in the place which Jehovah thy God doth fix on, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy handmaid, and the Levite who [is] within thy gates, and thou hast rejoiced before Jehovah thy God in every putting forth of thy hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: take heed to thee lest thou forsake the Levite all thy days on thy ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When Jehovah thy God doth enlarge thy border, as He hath spoken to thee, and thou hast said, Let me eat flesh -- for thy soul desireth to eat flesh -- of all the desire of thy soul thou dost eat flesh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When the place is far from thee which Jehovah thy God doth choose to put His name there, then thou hast sacrificed of thy herd and of thy flock which Jehovah hath given to thee, as I have commanded thee, and hast eaten within thy gates, of all the desire of thy soul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only, as the roe and the hart is eaten, so dost thou eat it; the unclean and the clean doth alike eat it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, be sure not to eat the blood, for the blood [is] the life, and thou dost not eat the life with the flesh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not eat it, on the earth thou dost pour it as water;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not eat it, in order that it may be well with thee, and with thy sons after thee, when thou dost that which [is] right in the eyes of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, thy holy things which thou hast, and thy vows, thou dost take up, and hast gone in unto the place which Jehovah doth choose,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast made thy burnt-offerings -- the flesh and the blood -- on the altar of Jehovah thy God; and the blood of thy sacrifices is poured out by the altar of Jehovah thy God, and the flesh thou dost eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Observe, and thou hast obeyed all these words which I am commanding thee, in order that it may be well with thee and with thy sons after thee -- to the age, when thou dost that which [is] good and right in the eyes of Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When Jehovah thy God doth cut off the nations -- whither thou art going in to possess them -- from thy presence, and thou hast possessed them, and hast dwelt in their land --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: take heed to thee, lest thou be snared after them, after their being destroyed out of thy presence, and lest thou enquire about their gods, saying, How do these nations serve their gods, and I do so -- even I?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not do so to Jehovah thy God; for every abomination of Jehovah which He is hating they have done to their gods, for even their sons and their daughters they burn with fire to their gods.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The whole thing which I am commanding you -- it ye observe to do; thou dost not add unto it, nor diminish from it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there ariseth in your midst a prophet, or a dreamer of a dream, and he hath given unto thee a sign or wonder,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sign and the wonder hath come which he hath spoken of unto thee, saying, Let us go after other gods (which thou hast not known), and serve them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not hearken unto the words of that prophet, or unto that dreamer of the dream, for Jehovah your God is trying you, to know whether ye are loving Jehovah your God with all your heart, and with all your soul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: after Jehovah your God ye walk, and Him ye fear, and His commands ye keep, and to His voice ye hearken, and Him ye serve, and to Him ye cleave.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that prophet, or that dreamer of the dream, is put to death, for he hath spoken apostasy against Jehovah your God (who is bringing you out of the land of Egypt, and hath ransomed you out of a house of servants), to drive you out of the way in which Jehovah thy God hath commanded thee to walk, and thou hast put away the evil thing from thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thy brother -- son of thy mother, or thy son, or thy daughter, or the wife of thy bosom, or thy friend who [is] as thine own soul -- doth move thee, in secret, saying, Let us go and serve other gods -- (which thou hast not known, thou and thy fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of the gods of the peoples who [are] round about you, who are near unto thee, or who are far off from thee, from the end of the earth even unto the end of the earth) --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not consent to him, nor hearken unto him, nor doth thine eye have pity on him, nor dost thou spare, nor dost thou cover him over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But thou dost surely kill him; thy hand is on him, in the first place, to put him to death, and the hand of all the people last;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast stoned him with stones, and he hath died, for he hath sought to drive thee away from Jehovah thy God, who is bringing thee out of the land of Egypt, out of a house of servants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all Israel do hear and fear, and add not to do like this evil thing in thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou hearest, in one of thy cities which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee to dwell there, [one] saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Men, sons of worthlessness, have gone out of thy midst, and they force away the inhabitants of their city, saying, Let us go and serve other gods, which ye have not known --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast enquired, and searched, and asked diligently, and lo, truth; the thing is established; this abomination hath been done in thy midst:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost surely smite the inhabitants of that city by the mouth of the sword; devoting it, and all that [is] in it, even its cattle, by the mouth of the sword;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all its spoil thou dost gather unto the midst of its broad place, and hast burned with fire the city and all its spoil completely, before Jehovah thy God, and it hath been a heap age-during, it is not built any more;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there doth not cleave to thy hand any of the devoted thing, so that Jehovah doth turn back from the fierceness of His anger, and hath given to thee mercies, and loved thee, and multiplied thee, as He hath sworn to thy fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when thou dost hearken to the voice of Jehovah thy God, to keep all his commands which I am commanding thee to-day, to do that which [is] right in the eyes of Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Sons ye [are] to Jehovah your God; ye do not cut yourselves, nor make baldness between your eyes for the dead;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a holy people [art] thou to Jehovah thy God, and on thee hath Jehovah fixed to be to Him for a people, a peculiar treasure, out of all the peoples who [are] on the face of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not eat any abominable thing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'this [is] the beast which ye do eat: ox, lamb of the sheep, or kid of the goats,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: hart, and roe, and fallow deer, and wild goat, and pygarg, and wild ox, and chamois;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every beast dividing the hoof, and cleaving the cleft into two hoofs, bringing up the cud, among the beasts -- it ye do eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, this ye do not eat, of those bringing up the cud, and of those dividing the cloven hoof: the camel, and the hare, and the rabbit, for they are bringing up the cud but the hoof have not divided; unclean they [are] to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sow, for it is dividing the hoof, and not [bringing] up the cud, unclean it [is] to you; of their flesh ye do not eat, and against their carcase ye do not come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This ye do eat of all that [are] in the waters; all that hath fins and scales ye do eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and anything which hath not fins and scales ye do not eat; unclean it [is] to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Any clean bird ye do eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these [are] they of which ye do not eat: the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the ospray,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the glede, and the kite, and the vulture after its kind,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every raven after its kind;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the owl, and the night-hawk, and the cuckoo, and the hawk after its kind;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [little] owl, and the [great] owl, and the swan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the pelican, and the gier-eagle, and the cormorant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the stork, and the heron after its kind, and the lapwing, and the bat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every teeming thing which is flying, unclean it [is] to you; they are not eaten;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: any clean fowl ye do eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye do not eat of any carcase; to the sojourner who [is] within thy gates thou dost give it, and he hath eaten it; or sell [it] to a stranger; for a holy people thou [art] to Jehovah thy God; thou dost not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost certainly tithe all the increase of thy seed which the field is bringing forth year by year;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast eaten before Jehovah thy God, in the place where He doth choose to cause His name to tabernacle, the tithe of thy corn, of thy new wine, and of thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herd, and of thy flock, so that thou dost learn to fear Jehovah thy God all the days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when the way is too much for thee, that thou art not able to carry it -- when the place is too far off from thee which Jehovah thy God doth choose to put His name there, when Jehovah thy God doth bless thee; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast given [it] in money, and hast bound up the money in thy hand, and gone unto the place on which Jehovah thy God doth fix;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast given the money for any thing which thy soul desireth, for oxen, and for sheep, and for wine, and for strong drink, and for any thing which thy soul asketh, and thou hast eaten there before Jehovah thy God, and thou hast rejoiced, thou and thy house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to the Levite who [is] within thy gates, thou dost not forsake him, for he hath no portion and inheritance with thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'At the end of three years thou dost bring out all the tithe of thine increase in that year, and hast placed [it] within thy gates;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and come in hath the Levite (for he hath no part and inheritance with thee), and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, who [are] within thy gates, and they have eaten, and been satisfied, so that Jehovah thy God doth bless thee in all the work of thy hand which thou dost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'At the end of seven years thou dost make a release,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this [is] the matter of the release: Every owner of a loan [is] to release his hand which he doth lift up against his neighbour, he doth not exact of his neighbour and of his brother, but hath proclaimed a release to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of the stranger thou mayest exact, and that which is thine with thy brother doth thy hand release;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only when there is no needy one with thee, for Jehovah doth greatly bless thee in the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, if thou dost diligently hearken to the voice of Jehovah thy God, to observe to do all this command which I am commanding thee to-day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee as He hath spoken to thee; and thou hast lent [to] many nations, and thou hast not borrowed; and thou hast ruled over many nations, and over thee they do not rule.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there is with thee any needy one of one of thy brethren, in one of thy cities, in thy land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, thou dost not harden thy heart, nor shut thy hand from thy needy brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thou dost certainly open thy hand to him, and dost certainly lend him sufficient for his lack which he lacketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to thee lest there be a word in thy heart -- worthless, saying, Near [is] the seventh year, the year of release; and thine eye is evil against thy needy brother, and thou dost not give to him, and he hath called concerning thee unto Jehovah, and it hath been in thee sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost certainly give to him, and thy heart is not sad in thy giving to him, for because of this thing doth Jehovah thy God bless thee in all thy works, and in every putting forth of thy hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because the needy one doth not cease out of the land, therefore I am commanding thee, saying, Thou dost certainly open thy hand to thy brother, to thy poor, and to thy needy one, in thy land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thy brother is sold to thee, a Hebrew or a Hebrewess, and he hath served thee six years -- then in the seventh year thou dost send him away free from thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when thou dost send him away free from thee, thou dost not send him away empty;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost certainly encircle him out of thy flock, and out of thy threshing-floor, and out of thy wine-vat; [of] that which Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee thou dost give to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered that a servant thou hast been in the land of Egypt, and Jehovah thy God doth ransom thee; therefore I am commanding thee this thing to-day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when he saith unto thee, I go not out from thee -- because he hath loved thee, and thy house, because [it is] good for him with thee --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast taken the awl, and hast put [it] through his ear, and through the door, and he hath been to thee a servant age-during; and also to thy handmaid thou dost do so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'It is not hard in thine eyes, in thy sending him away free from thee; for the double of the hire of an hireling he hath served thee six years, and Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee in all that thou dost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Every firstling that is born in thy herd and in thy flock -- the male thou dost sanctify to Jehovah thy God; thou dost not work with the firstling of thine ox, nor shear the firstling of thy flock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: before Jehovah thy God thou dost eat it year by year, in the place which Jehovah doth choose, thou and thy house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when there is in it a blemish, lame, or blind, any evil blemish, thou dost not sacrifice it to Jehovah thy God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: within thy gates thou dost eat it, the unclean and the clean alike, as the roe, and as the hart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, its blood thou dost not eat; on the earth thou dost pour it as water.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Observe the month of Abib -- and thou hast made a passover to Jehovah thy God, for in the month of Abib hath Jehovah thy God brought thee out of Egypt by night;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast sacrificed a passover to Jehovah thy God, of the flock, and of the herd, in the place which Jehovah doth choose to cause His name to tabernacle there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not eat with it any fermented thing, seven days thou dost eat with it unleavened things, bread of affliction; for in haste thou hast come out of the land of Egypt; so that thou dost remember the day of thy coming out of the land of Egypt all days of thy life;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is not seen with thee leaven in all thy border seven days, and there doth not remain of the flesh which thou dost sacrifice at evening on the first day till morning.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou art not able to sacrifice the passover within any of thy gates which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: except at the place which Jehovah thy God doth choose to cause His name to tabernacle -- there thou dost sacrifice the passover in the evening, at the going in of the sun, the season of thy coming out of Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast cooked and eaten in the place on which Jehovah thy God doth fix, and hast turned in the morning, and gone to thy tents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: six days thou dost eat unleavened things, and on the seventh day [is] a restraint to Jehovah thy God; thou dost do no work.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Seven weeks thou dost number to thee; from the beginning of the sickle among the standing corn thou dost begin to number seven weeks,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast made the feast of weeks to Jehovah thy God, a tribute of a free-will offering of thy hand, which thou dost give, as Jehovah thy God doth bless thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast rejoiced before Jehovah thy God, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy handmaid, and the Levite who [is] within thy gates, and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, who [are] in thy midst, in the place which Jehovah thy God doth choose to cause His name to tabernacle there,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered that a servant thou hast been in Egypt, and hast observed and done these statutes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The feast of booths thou dost make for thee seven days, in thine in-gathering of thy threshing-floor, and of thy wine-vat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast rejoiced in thy feast, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy man-servant, and thy handmaid, and the Levite, and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, who [are] within thy gates.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Seven days thou dost feast before Jehovah thy God, in the place which Jehovah doth choose, for Jehovah thy God doth bless thee in all thine increase, and in every work of thy hands, and thou hast been only rejoicing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Three times in a year doth every one of thy males appear before Jehovah thy God in the place which He doth choose -- in the feast of unleavened things, and in the feast of weeks, and in the feast of booths; and they do not appear before Jehovah empty;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: each according to the gift of his hand, according to the blessing of Jehovah thy God, which He hath given to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Judges and authorities thou dost make to thee within all thy gates which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, for thy tribes; and they have judged the people -- a righteous judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost not turn aside judgment; thou dost not discern faces, nor take a bribe, for the bribe blindeth the eyes of the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Righteousness -- righteousness thou dost pursue, so that thou livest, and hast possessed the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not plant for thee a shrine of any trees near the altar of Jehovah thy God, which thou makest for thyself,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou dost not raise up to thee any standing image which Jehovah thy God is hating.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not sacrifice to Jehovah thy God ox or sheep in which there is a blemish -- any evil thing; for it [is] the abomination of Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there is found in thy midst, in one of thy cities which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, a man or a woman who doth the evil thing in the eyes of Jehovah thy God by transgressing His covenant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he doth go and serve other gods, and doth bow himself to them, and to the sun, or to the moon, or to any of the host of the heavens, which I have not commanded --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been declared to thee, and thou hast heard, and hast searched diligently, and lo, truth; the thing is established; this abomination hath been done in Israel --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then thou hast brought out that man, or that woman, who hath done this evil thing, unto thy gates -- the man or the woman -- and thou hast stoned them with stones, and they have died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the mouth of two witnesses or of three witnesses is he who is dead put to death; he is not put to death by the mouth of one witness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the hand of the witnesses is on him, in the first place, to put him to death, and the hand of all the people last; and thou hast put away the evil thing out of thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When anything is too hard for thee for judgment, between blood and blood, between plea and plea, and between stroke and stroke -- matters of strife within thy gates -- then thou hast risen, and gone up unto the place on which Jehovah thy God doth fix,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast come in unto the priests, the Levites, and unto the judge who is in those days, and hast inquired, and they have declared to thee the word of judgment,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast done according to the tenor of the word which they declare to thee ([they] of that place which Jehovah doth choose; and thou hast observed to do according to all that they direct thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'According to the tenor of the law which they direct thee, and according to the judgment which they say to thee thou dost do; thou dost not turn aside from the word which they declare to thee, right or left.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man who acteth with presumption, so as not to hearken unto the priest (who is standing to serve there Jehovah thy God), or unto the judge, even that man hath died, and thou hast put away the evil thing from Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the people do hear and fear, and do not presume any more.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou comest in unto the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, and hast possessed it, and dwelt in it, and thou hast said, Let me set over me a king like all the nations which [are] round about me, --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost certainly set over thee a king on whom Jehovah doth fix; from the midst of thy brethren thou dost set over thee a king; thou art not able to set over thee a stranger, who is not thy brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, he doth not multiply to himself horses, nor cause the people to turn back to Egypt, so as to multiply horses, seeing Jehovah hath said to you, Ye do not add to turn back in this way any more.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he doth not multiply to himself wives, and his heart doth not turn aside, and silver and gold he doth not multiply to himself -- exceedingly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when he sitteth on the throne of his kingdom, that he hath written for himself the copy of this law, on a book, from [that] before the priests the Levites,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been with him, and he hath read in it all days of his life, so that he doth learn to fear Jehovah his God, to keep all the words of this law, and these statutes, to do them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that his heart is not high above his brethren, and so as not to turn aside from the command, right or left, so that he prolongeth days over his kingdom, he and his sons, in the midst of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'There is not to the priests the Levites -- all the tribe of Levi -- a portion and inheritance with Israel; fire-offerings of Jehovah, even His inheritance, they eat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he hath no inheritance in the midst of his brethren; Jehovah Himself [is] his inheritance, as He hath spoken to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this is the priest's right from the people, from those sacrificing a sacrifice, whether ox or sheep, he hath even given to the priest the leg, and the two cheeks, and the stomach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the first of thy corn, of thy new wine, and of thine oil, and the first of the fleece of thy flock, thou dost give to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for on him hath Jehovah thy God fixed, out of all thy tribes, to stand to serve in the name of Jehovah, He and his sons continually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when the Levite cometh from one of thy cities out of all Israel, where he hath sojourned, and hath come with all the desire of his soul unto the place which Jehovah doth choose,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then he hath ministered in the name of Jehovah his God, like all his brethren, the Levites, who are standing there before Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: portion as portion they do eat, apart from his sold things, with the fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou art coming in unto the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, thou dost not learn to do according to the abominations of those nations:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: there is not found in thee one causing his son and his daughter to pass over into fire, a user of divinations, an observer of clouds, and an enchanter, and a sorcerer,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a charmer, and one asking at a familiar spirit, and a wizard, and one seeking unto the dead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For the abomination of Jehovah [is] every one doing these, and because of these abominations is Jehovah thy God dispossessing them from thy presence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Perfect thou art with Jehovah thy God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for these nations whom thou art possessing, unto observers of clouds, and unto diviners, do hearken; and thou -- not so hath Jehovah thy God suffered thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A prophet out of thy midst, out of thy brethren, like to me, doth Jehovah thy God raise up to thee -- unto him ye hearken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according to all that thou didst ask from Jehovah thy God, in Horeb, in the day of the assembly, saying, Let me not add to hear the voice of Jehovah my God, and this great fire let me not see any more, and I die not;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah saith unto me, They have done well that they have spoken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a prophet I raise up to them, out of the midst of their brethren, like to thee; and I have given my words in his mouth, and he hath spoken unto them all that which I command him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been -- the man who doth not hearken unto My words which he doth speak in My name, I require [it] of him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, the prophet who presumeth to speak a word in My name -- that which I have not commanded him to speak -- and who speaketh in the name of other gods -- even that prophet hath died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when thou sayest in thy heart, How do we know the word which Jehovah hath not spoken? --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that which the prophet speaketh in the name of Jehovah, and the thing is not, and cometh not -- it [is] the word which Jehovah hath not spoken; in presumption hath the prophet spoken it; -- thou art not afraid of him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When Jehovah thy God doth cut off the nations, whose land Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, and thou hast succeeded them, and dwelt in their cities, and in their houses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: three cities thou dost separate for thee in the midst of thy land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost prepare for thee the way, and hast divided into three parts the border of thy land which Jehovah thy God doth cause thee to inherit, and it hath been for the fleeing thither of every man-slayer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this [is] the matter of the man-slayer who fleeth thither, and hath lived: He who smiteth his neighbour unknowingly, and is not hating him heretofore,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: even he who cometh in with his neighbour into a forest to hew wood, and his hand hath driven with an axe to cut the tree, and the iron hath slipped from the wood, and hath met his neighbour, and he hath died -- he doth flee unto one of these cities, and hath lived,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest the redeemer of blood pursue after the man-slayer when his heart is hot, and hath overtaken him (because the way is great), and hath smitten him -- the life, and he hath no sentence of death, for he is not hating him heretofore;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore I am commanding thee, saying, Three cities thou dost separate to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if Jehovah thy God doth enlarge thy border, as He hath sworn to thy fathers, and hath given to thee all the land which He hath spoken to give to thy fathers --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when thou keepest all this command to do it, which I am commanding thee to-day, to love Jehovah thy God, and to walk in His ways all the days -- then thou hast added to thee yet three cities to these three;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and innocent blood is not shed in the midst of thy land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance, and there hath been upon thee blood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when a man is hating his neighbour, and hath lain in wait for him, and risen against him, and smitten him -- the life, and he hath died, and he hath fled unto one of these cities,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then the elders of his city have sent and taken him from thence, and given him into the hand of the redeemer of blood, and he hath died;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thine eye hath no pity on him, and thou hast put away the innocent blood from Israel, and it is well with thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not remove a border of thy neighbour, which they of former times have made, in thine inheritance, which thou dost inherit in the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'One witness doth not rise against a man for any iniquity, and for any sin, in any sin which he sinneth; by the mouth of two witnesses, or by the mouth of three witnesses, is a thing established.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a violent witness doth rise against a man, to testify against him apostasy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then have both the men who have the strife stood before Jehovah, before the priests and the judges who are in those days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the judges have searched diligently, and lo, the witness [is] a false witness, a falsehood he hath testified against his brother:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then ye have done to him as he devised to do to his brother, and thou hast put away the evil thing out of thy midst,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those who are left do hear and fear, and add not to do any more according to this evil thing in thy midst;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thine eye doth not pity -- life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou goest out to battle against thine enemy, and hast seen horse and chariot -- a people more numerous than thou -- thou art not afraid of them, for Jehovah thy God [is] with thee, who is bringing thee up out of the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, in your drawing near unto the battle, that the priest hath come nigh, and spoken unto the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and said unto them, Hear, Israel, ye are drawing near to-day to battle against your enemies, let not your hearts be tender, fear not, nor make haste, nor be terrified at their presence,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah your God [is] He who is going with you, to fight for you with your enemies -- to save you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the authorities have spoken unto the people, saying, Who [is] the man that hath built a new house, and hath not dedicated it? -- let him go and turn back to his house, lest he die in battle, and another man dedicate it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And who [is] the man that hath planted a vineyard, and hath not made it common? -- let him go and turn back to his house, lest he die in battle, and another man make it common.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And who [is] the man that hath betrothed a woman, and hath not taken her? -- let him go and turn back to his house, lest he die in battle, and another man take her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the authorities have added to speak unto the people, and said, Who [is] the man that is afraid and tender of heart? -- let him go and turn back to his house, and the heart of his brethren doth not melt like his heart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass as the authorities finish to speak unto the people, that they have appointed princes of the hosts at the head of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou drawest near unto a city to fight against it, then thou hast called unto it for Peace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, if Peace it answer thee, and hath opened to thee, then it hath come to pass -- all the people who are found in it are to thee for tributaries, and have served thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if it doth not make peace with thee, and hath made with thee war, then thou hast laid siege against it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath given it into thy hand, and thou hast smitten every male of it by the mouth of the sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, the women, and the infants, and the cattle, and all that is in the city, all its spoil, thou dost seize for thyself, and thou hast eaten the spoil of thine enemies which Jehovah thy God hath given to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So thou dost do to all the cities which are very far off from thee, which are not of the cities of these nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, of the cities of these peoples which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee [for] an inheritance, thou dost not keep alive any breathing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thou dost certainly devote the Hittite, and the Amorite, the Canaanite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, as Jehovah thy God hath commanded thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that they teach you not to do according to all their abominations which they have done to their gods, and ye have sinned against Jehovah your God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou layest siege unto a city many days, to fight against it, to capture it, thou dost not destroy its trees to force an axe against them, for of them thou dost eat, and them thou dost not cut down -- for man's [is] the tree of the field -- to go in at thy presence in the siege.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, the tree, which thou knowest that it [is] not a fruit-tree, it thou dost destroy, and hast cut down, and hast built a bulwark against the city which is making with thee war till thou hast subdued it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When one is found slain on the ground which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee to possess it -- fallen in a field -- it is not known who hath smitten him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then have thine elders and thy judges gone out and measured unto the cities which [are] round about the slain one,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, the city which [is] near unto the slain one, even the elders of that city have taken a heifer of the herd, which hath not been wrought with, which hath not drawn in the yoke,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the elders of that city have brought down the heifer unto a hard valley, which is not tilled nor sown, and have beheaded there the heifer in the valley.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the priests, sons of Levi, have come nigh -- for on them hath Jehovah thy God fixed to serve Him, and to bless in the name of Jehovah, and by their mouth is every strife, and every stroke --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the elders of that city, who are near unto the slain one, do wash their hands over the heifer which is beheaded in the valley,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have answered and said, Our hands have not shed this blood, and our eyes have not seen --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: receive atonement for Thy people Israel, whom Thou hast ransomed, O Jehovah, and suffer not innocent blood in the midst of Thy people Israel; and the blood hath been pardoned to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou dost put away the innocent blood out of thy midst, for thou dost that which [is] right in the eyes of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou goest out to battle against thine enemies, and Jehovah thy God hath given them into thy hand, and thou hast taken captive its captivity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast seen in the captivity a woman of fair form, and hast delighted in her, and hast taken to thee for a wife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast brought her in unto the midst of thy household, and she hath shaved her head, and prepared her nails,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and turned aside the raiment of her captivity from off her, and hath dwelt in thy house, and bewailed her father and her mother a month of days, and afterwards thou dost go in unto her and hast married her, and she hath been to thee for a wife:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been -- if thou hast not delighted in her, that thou hast sent her away at her desire, and thou dost not at all sell her for money; thou dost not tyrannize over her, because that thou hast humbled her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man hath two wives, the one loved and the other hated, and they have borne to him sons (the loved one and the hated one), and the first-born son hath been to the hated one;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it hath been, in the day of his causing his sons to inherit that which he hath, he is not able to declare first-born the son of the loved one, in the face of the son of the hated one -- the first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But the first-born, son of the hated one, he doth acknowledge, to give to him a double portion of all that is found with him, for he [is] the beginning of his strength; to him [is] the right of the first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man hath a son apostatizing and rebellious -- he is not hearkening to the voice of his father, and to the voice of his mother, and they have chastised him, and he doth not hearken unto them --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then laid hold on him have his father and his mother, and they have brought him out unto the elders of his city, and unto the gate of his place,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and have said unto the elders of his city, Our son -- this one -- is apostatizing and rebellious; he is not hearkening to our voice -- a glutton and drunkard;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the men of his city have stoned him with stones, and he hath died, and thou hast put away the evil out of thy midst, and all Israel do hear and fear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when there is in a man a sin -- a cause of death, and he hath been put to death, and thou hast hanged him on a tree,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: his corpse doth not remain on the tree, for thou dost certainly bury him in that day -- for a thing lightly esteemed of God [is] the hanged one -- and thou dost not defile thy ground which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not see the ox of thy brother or his sheep driven away, and hast hidden thyself from them, thou dost certainly turn them back to thy brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if thy brother [is] not near unto thee, and thou hast not known him, then thou hast removed it unto the midst of thy house, and it hath been with thee till thy brother seek it, and thou hast given it back to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and so thou dost to his ass, and so thou dost to his garment, and so thou dost to any lost thing of thy brother's, which is lost by him, and thou hast found it; thou art not able to hide thyself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not see the ass of thy brother, or his ox, falling in the way, and hast hid thyself from them; thou dost certainly raise [them] up with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The habiliments of a man are not on a woman, nor doth a man put on the garment of a woman, for the abomination of Jehovah thy God [is] any one doing these.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a bird's nest cometh before thee in the way, in any tree, or on the earth, brood or eggs, and the mother sitting on the brood or on the eggs, thou dost not take the mother with the young ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost certainly send away the mother, and the young ones dost take to thyself, so that it is well with thee, and thou hast prolonged days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou buildest a new house, then thou hast made a parapet to thy roof, and thou dost not put blood on thy house when one falleth from it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not sow thy vineyard [with] divers things, lest the fulness of the seed which thou dost sow, and the increase of the vineyard, be separated.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not plow with an ox and with an ass together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not put on a mixed cloth, wool and linen together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Fringes thou dost make to thee on the four skirts of thy covering with which thou dost cover [thyself].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man taketh a wife, and hath gone in unto her, and hated her,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and laid against her actions of words, and brought out against her an evil name, and said, This woman I have taken, and I draw near unto her, and I have not found in her tokens of virginity:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then hath the father of the damsel -- and her mother -- taken and brought out the tokens of virginity of the damsel unto the elders of the city in the gate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the father of the damsel hath said unto the elders, My daughter I have given to this man for a wife, and he doth hate her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, he hath laid actions of words, saying, I have not found to thy daughter tokens of virginity -- and these [are] the tokens of the virginity of my daughter! and they have spread out the garment before the elders of the city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the elders of that city have taken the man, and chastise him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and fined him a hundred silverlings, and given to the father of the damsel, because he hath brought out an evil name on a virgin of Israel, and she is to him for a wife, he is not able to send her away all his days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if this thing hath been truth -- tokens of virginity have not been found for the damsel --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then they have brought out the damsel unto the opening of her father's house, and stoned her have the men of her city with stones, and she hath died, for she hath done folly in Israel, to go a-whoring [in] her father's house; and thou hast put away the evil thing out of thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man is found lying with a woman, married to a husband, then they have died even both of them, the man who is lying with the woman, also the woman; and thou hast put away the evil thing out of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there is a damsel, a virgin, betrothed to a man, and a man hath found her in a city, and lain with her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then ye have brought them both out unto the gate of that city, and stoned them with stones, and they have died: -- the damsel, because that she hath not cried, [being] in a city; and the man, because that he hath humbled his neighbour's wife; and thou hast put away the evil thing out of thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if in a field the man find the damsel who is betrothed, and the man hath laid hold on her, and lain with her, then hath the man who hath lain with her died alone;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to the damsel thou dost not do anything, the damsel hath no deadly sin; for as a man riseth against his neighbour and hath murdered him -- the life, so [is] this thing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in a field he found her, she hath cried -- the damsel who is betrothed -- and she hath no saviour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man findeth a damsel, a virgin who is not betrothed, and hath caught her, and lain with her, and they have been found,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then hath the man who is lying with her given to the father of the damsel fifty silverlings, and to him she is for a wife; because that he hath humbled her, he is not able to send her away all his days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A man doth not take his father's wife, nor uncover his father's skirt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'One wounded, bruised, or cut in the member doth not enter into the assembly of Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a bastard doth not enter into the assembly of Jehovah; even a tenth generation of him doth not enter into the assembly of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'An Ammonite and a Moabite doth not enter into the assembly of Jehovah; even a tenth generation of them doth not enter into the assembly of Jehovah -- to the age;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because that they have not come before you with bread and with water in the way, in your coming out from Egypt, and because he hath hired against thee Balaam son of Beor, of Pethor of Aram-Naharaim, to revile thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath not been willing to hearken unto Balaam, and Jehovah thy God doth turn for thee the reviling to a blessing, because Jehovah thy God hath loved thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not seek their peace and their good all thy days -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not abominate an Edomite, for thy brother he [is]; thou dost not abominate an Egyptian, for a sojourner thou hast been in his land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sons who are begotten of them, a third generation of them, doth enter into the assembly of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a camp goeth out against thine enemies, then thou hast kept from every evil thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there is in thee a man who is not clean, from an accident at night -- then he hath gone out unto the outside of the camp -- he doth not come in unto the midst of the camp --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, at the turning of the evening, he doth bathe with water, and at the going in of the sun he doth come in unto the midst of the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And a station thou hast at the outside of the camp, and thou hast gone out thither without,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a nail thou hast on thy staff, and it hath been, in thy sitting without, that thou hast digged with it, and turned back, and covered thy filth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah thy God is walking up and down in the midst of thy camp, to deliver thee, and to give thine enemies before thee, and thy camp hath been holy, and He doth not see in thee the nakedness of anything, and hath turned back from after thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not shut up a servant unto his lord, who is delivered unto thee from his lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with thee he doth dwell, in thy midst, in the place which he chooseth within one of thy gates, where it is pleasing to him; thou dost not oppress him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'There is not a whore among the daughters of Israel, nor is there a whoremonger among the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost not bring a gift of a whore, or a price of a dog, into the house of Jehovah thy God, for any vow; for the abomination of Jehovah thy God [are] even both of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not lend in usury to thy brother; usury of money, usury of food, usury of anything which is lent on usury.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To a stranger thou mayest lend in usury, and to thy brother thou dost not lend in usury, so that Jehovah thy God doth bless thee in every putting forth of thy hand on the land whither thou goest in to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou vowest a vow to Jehovah thy God, thou dost not delay to complete it; for Jehovah thy God doth certainly require it from thee, and it hath been in thee -- sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when thou forbearest to vow, it is not in thee a sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The produce of thy lips thou dost keep, and hast done [it], as thou hast vowed to Jehovah thy God; a free-will-offering, which thou hast spoken with thy mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou comest in unto the vineyard of thy neighbour, then thou hast eaten grapes, according to thy desire, thy sufficiency; but into thy vessel thou dost not put [any].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When thou comest in among the standing-corn of thy neighbour, then thou hast plucked the ears with thy hand, but a sickle thou dost not wave over the standing-corn of thy neighbour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man doth take a wife, and hath married her, and it hath been, if she doth not find grace in his eyes (for he hath found in her nakedness of anything), and he hath written for her a writing of divorce, and given [it] into her hand, and sent her out of his house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she hath gone out of his house, and hath gone and been another man's,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the latter man hath hated her, and written for her a writing of divorce, and given [it] into her hand, and sent her out of his house, or when the latter man dieth, who hath taken her to himself for a wife:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Her former husband who sent her away is not able to turn back to take her to be to him for a wife, after that she hath become defiled; for an abomination it [is] before Jehovah, and thou dost not cause the land to sin which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man taketh a new wife, he doth not go out into the host, and [one] doth not pass over unto him for anything; free he is at his own house one year, and hath rejoiced his wife whom he hath taken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'None doth take in pledge millstones, and rider, for life it [is] he is taking in pledge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man is found stealing a person, of his brethren, of the sons of Israel, and hath tyrannized over him, and sold him, then hath that thief died, and thou hast put away the evil thing out of thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed, in the plague of leprosy, to watch greatly, and to do according to all that the priests, the Levites, teach you; as I have commanded them ye observe to do;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: remember that which Jehovah thy God hath done to Miriam in the way, in your coming out of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou liftest up on thy brother a debt of anything, thou dost not go in unto his house to obtain his pledge;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: at the outside thou dost stand, and the man on whom thou art lifting [it] up is bringing out unto thee the pledge at the outside.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if he is a poor man, thou dost not lie down with his pledge;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost certainly give back to him the pledge at the going in of the sun, and he hath lain down in his own raiment, and hath blessed thee; and to thee it is righteousness before Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not oppress a hireling, poor and needy, of thy brethren or of thy sojourner who is in thy land within thy gates;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in his day thou dost give his hire, and the sun doth not go in upon it, for he [is] poor, and unto it he is lifting up his soul, and he doth not cry against thee unto Jehovah, and it hath been in thee -- sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Fathers are not put to death for sons, and sons are not put to death for fathers -- each for his own sin, they are put to death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not turn aside the judgment of a fatherless sojourner, nor take in pledge the garment of a widow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered that a servant thou hast been in Egypt, and Jehovah thy God doth ransom thee from thence; therefore I am commanding thee to do this thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou reapest thy harvest in thy field, and hast forgotten a sheaf in a field, thou dost not turn back to take it; to the sojourner, to the fatherless, and to the widow, it is; so that Jehovah thy God doth bless thee in all the work of thy hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou beatest thine olive, thou dost not examine the branch behind thee; to the sojourner, to the fatherless, and to the widow, it is.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou cuttest thy vineyard, thou dost not glean behind thee; to the sojourner, to the fatherless, and to the widow, it is;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast remembered that a servant thou hast been in the land of Egypt; therefore I am commanding thee to do this thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When there is a strife between men, and they have come nigh unto the judgment, and they have judged, and declared righteous the righteous, and declared wrong the wrong-doer,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it hath come to pass, if the wrong-doer is to be smitten, that the judge hath caused him to fall down, and [one] hath smitten him in his presence, according to the sufficiency of his wrong-doing, by number;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: forty [times] he doth smite him -- he is not adding, lest, he is adding to smite him above these -- many stripes, and thy brother is lightly esteemed in thine eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou dost not muzzle an ox in its threshing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When brethren dwell together, and one of them hath died, and hath no son, the wife of the dead is not without to a strange man; her husband's brother doth go in unto her, and hath taken her to him for a wife, and doth perform the duty of her husband's brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, the first-born which she beareth doth rise for the name of his dead brother, and his name is not wiped away out of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if the man doth not delight to take his brother's wife, then hath his brother's wife gone up to the gate, unto the elders, and said, My husband's brother is refusing to raise up to his brother a name in Israel; he hath not been willing to perform the duty of my husband's brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the elders of his city have called for him, and spoken unto him, and he hath stood and said, I have no desire to take her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then hath his brother's wife drawn nigh unto him, before the eyes of the elders, and drawn his shoe from off his foot, and spat in his face, and answered and said, Thus it is done to the man who doth not build up the house of his brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his name hath been called in Israel -- The house of him whose shoe is drawn off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When men strive together, one with another, and the wife of the one hath drawn near to deliver her husband out of the hand of his smiter, and hath put forth her hand, and laid hold on his secrets,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast cut off her hand, thine eye doth not spare.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou hast not in thy bag a stone and a stone, a great and a small.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast not in thy house an ephah and an ephah, a great and a small.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast a stone complete and just, thou hast an ephah complete and just, so that they prolong thy days on the ground which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the abomination of Jehovah thy God [is] any one doing these things, any one doing iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Remember that which Amalek hath done to thee in the way, in your going out from Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that he hath met thee in the way, and smiteth in all those feeble behind thee (and thou wearied and fatigued), and is not fearing God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it hath been, in Jehovah thy God's giving rest to thee, from all thine enemies round about, in the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance to possess it -- thou dost blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under the heavens -- thou dost not forget.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when thou comest in unto the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- an inheritance, and thou hast possessed it, and dwelt in it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that thou hast taken of the first of all the fruits of the ground which thou dost bring in out of thy land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, and hast put [it] in a basket, and gone unto the place which Jehovah thy God doth choose to cause His name to tabernacle there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast come in unto the priest who is in those days, and hast said unto him, I have declared to-day to Jehovah thy God, that I have come in unto the land which Jehovah hath sworn to our fathers to give to us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the priest hath taken the basket out of thy hand, and placed it before the altar of Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast answered and said before Jehovah thy God, A perishing Aramaean [is] my father! and he goeth down to Egypt, and sojourneth there with few men, and becometh there a nation, great, mighty, and numerous;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Egyptians do us evil, and afflict us, and put on us hard service;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we cry unto Jehovah, God of our fathers, and Jehovah heareth our voice, and seeth our affliction, and our labour, and our oppression;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah bringeth us out from Egypt, by a strong hand, and by a stretched-out arm, and by great fear, and by signs, and by wonders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he bringeth us in unto this place, and giveth to us this land -- a land flowing with milk and honey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, lo, I have brought in the first of the fruits of the ground which thou hast given to me, O Jehovah; -- and thou hast placed it before Jehovah thy God, and bowed thyself before Jehovah thy God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and rejoiced in all the good which Jehovah thy God hath given to thee, and to thy house, thou, and the Levite, and the sojourner who [is] in thy midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou dost complete to tithe all the tithe of thine increase in the third year, the year of the tithe, then thou hast given to the Levite, to the sojourner, to the fatherless, and to the widow, and they have eaten within thy gates, and been satisfied,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast said before Jehovah thy God, I have put away the separated thing out of the house, and also have given it to the Levite, and to the sojourner, and to the orphan, and to the widow, according to all Thy command which Thou hast commanded me; I have not passed over from Thy commands, nor have I forgotten.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have not eaten in mine affliction of it, nor have I put away of it for uncleanness, nor have I given of it for the dead; I have hearkened to the voice of Jehovah my God; I have done according to all that Thou hast commanded me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: look from Thy holy habitation, from the heavens, and bless Thy people Israel, and the ground which Thou hast given to us, as Thou hast sworn to our fathers -- a land flowing [with] milk and honey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This day Jehovah thy God is commanding thee to do these statutes and judgments; and thou hast hearkened and done them with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah thou hast caused to promise to-day to become thy God, and to walk in His ways, and to keep His statutes, and His commands, and His judgments, and to hearken to His voice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah hath caused thee to promise to-day to become His people, a peculiar treasure, as He hath spoken to thee, and to keep all His commands;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so as to make thee uppermost above all the nations whom He hath made for a praise, and for a name, and for beauty, and for thy being a holy people to Jehovah thy God, as He hath spoken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Moses -- the elders of Israel also -- commandeth the people, saying, Keep all the command which I am commanding you to-day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, in the day that ye pass over the Jordan unto the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee, that thou hast raised up for thee great stones, and plaistered them with plaister,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and written on them all the words of this law in thy passing over, so that thou goest in unto the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee -- a land flowing with milk and honey, as Jehovah, God of thy fathers, hath spoken to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, in your passing over the Jordan, ye raise up these stones which I am commanding you to-day, in mount Ebal, and thou hast plaistered them with plaister,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and built there an altar to Jehovah thy God, an altar of stones, thou dost not wave over them iron.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of complete stones thou buildest the altar of Jehovah thy God, and hast caused to ascend on it burnt-offerings to Jehovah thy God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and sacrificed peace-offerings, and eaten there, and rejoiced before Jehovah thy God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and written on the stones all the words of this law, well engraved.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh -- the priests, the Levites, also -- unto all Israel, saying, 'Keep silent, and hear, O Israel, this day thou hast become a people to Jehovah thy God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast hearkened to the voice of Jehovah thy God, and done His commands, and His statutes, which I am commanding thee to-day.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses commandeth the people on that day, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These do stand, to bless the people, on mount Gerizzim, in your passing over the Jordan: Simeon, and Levi, and Judah, and Issachar, and Joseph, and Benjamin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these do stand, for the reviling, on mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad, and Asher, and Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the Levites have answered and said unto every man of Israel -- a loud voice:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] the man who maketh a graven and molten image, the abomination of Jehovah, work of the hands of an artificer, and hath put [it] in a secret place, -- and all the people have answered and said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] He who is making light of his father and his mother, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is removing his neighbour's border, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is causing the blind to err in the way, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is turning aside the judgment of fatherless, sojourner, and widow, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is lying with his father's wife, for he hath uncovered his father's skirt, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is lying with any beast, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is lying with his sister, daughter of his father, or daughter of his mother, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is lying with his mother-in-law, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is smiting his neighbour in secret, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who is taking a bribe to smite a person, innocent blood, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] he who doth not establish the words of this law, to do them, -- and all the people have said, Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, if thou dost hearken diligently to the voice of Jehovah thy God, to observe to do all His commands which I am commanding thee to-day, that Jehovah thy God hath made thee uppermost above all the nations of the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all these blessings have come upon thee, and overtaken thee, because thou dost hearken to the voice of Jehovah thy God:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed [art] thou in the city, and blessed [art] thou in the field.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed [is] the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, and the fruit of thy cattle, increase of thine oxen, and wealth of thy flock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed [is] thy basket and thy kneading-trough.
