translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed [art] thou in thy coming in, and blessed [art] thou in thy going out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah giveth thine enemies, who are rising up against thee -- smitten before thy face; in one way they come out unto thee, and in seven ways they flee before thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah commandeth with thee the blessing in thy storehouses, and in every putting forth of thy hand, and hath blessed thee in the land which Jehovah thy God is giving to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth establish thee to Himself for a holy people, as He hath sworn to thee, when thou keepest the commands of Jehovah thy God, and hast walked in His ways;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the peoples of the land have seen that the name of Jehovah is called upon thee, and they have been afraid of thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah hath made thee abundant in good, in the fruit of the womb, and in the fruit of thy cattle, and in the fruit of thy ground, on the ground which Jehovah hath sworn to thy fathers to give to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth open to thee his good treasure -- the heavens -- to give the rain of thy land in its season, and to bless all the work of thy hand, and thou hast lent to many nations, and thou -- thou dost not borrow.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah hath given thee for head, and not for tail; and thou hast been only above, and art not beneath, for thou dost hearken unto the commands of Jehovah thy God, which I am commanding thee to-day, to keep and to do,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou dost not turn aside from all the words which I am commanding you to-day -- right or left -- to go after other gods, to serve them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, if thou dost not hearken unto the voice of Jehovah thy God to observe to do all His commands, and His statutes, which I am commanding thee to-day, that all these revilings have come upon thee, and overtaken thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [art] thou in the city, and cursed [art] thou in the field.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] thy basket and thy kneading-trough.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [is] the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy land, increase of thine oxen, and wealth of thy flock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cursed [art] thou in thy coming in, and cursed [art] thou in thy going out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth send on thee the curse, the trouble, and the rebuke, in every putting forth of thy hand which thou dost, till thou art destroyed, and till thou perish hastily, because of the evil of thy doings [by] which thou hast forsaken Me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth cause to cleave to thee the pestilence, till He consume thee from off the ground whither thou art going in to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth smite thee with consumption, and with fever, and with inflammation, and with extreme burning, and with sword, and with blasting, and with mildew, and they have pursued thee till thou perish

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thy heavens which [are] over thy head have been brass, and the earth which [is] under thee iron;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah giveth the rain of thy land -- dust and ashes; from the heavens it cometh down on thee till thou art destroyed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah giveth thee smitten before thine enemies; in one way thou goest out unto them, and in seven ways dost flee before them, and thou hast been for a trembling to all kingdoms of the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy carcase hath been for food to every fowl of the heavens, and to the beast of the earth, and there is none causing trembling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth smite thee with the ulcer of Egypt, and with emerods, and with scurvy, and with itch, of which thou art not able to be healed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth smite thee with madness, and with blindness, and with astonishment of heart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast been gropling at noon, as the blind gropeth in darkness; and thou dost not cause thy ways to prosper; and thou hast been only oppressed and plundered all the days, and there is no saviour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A woman thou dost betroth, and another man doth lie with her; a house thou dost build, and dost not dwell in it; a vineyard thou dost plant, and dost not make it common;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thine ox [is] slaughtered before thine eyes, and thou dost not eat of it; thine ass [is] taken violently away from before thee, and it is not given back to thee; thy sheep [are] given to thine enemies, and there is no saviour for thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thy sons and thy daughters [are] given to another people, and thine eyes are looking and consuming for them all the day, and thy hand is not to God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The fruit of thy ground, and all thy labour, eat up doth a people whom thou hast not known; and thou hast been only oppressed and bruised all the days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast been mad, because of the sight of thine eyes which thou dost see.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth smite thee with an evil ulcer, on the knees, and on the legs (of which thou art not able to be healed), from the sole of thy foot even unto thy crown.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth cause thee to go, and thy king whom thou raisest up over thee, unto a nation which thou hast not known, thou and thy fathers, and thou hast served there other gods, wood and stone;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast been for an astonishment, for a simile, and for a byword among all the peoples whither Jehovah doth lead thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Much seed thou dost take out into the field, and little thou dost gather in, for the locust doth consume it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: vineyards thou dost plant, and hast laboured, and wine thou dost not drink nor gather, for the worm doth consume it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: olives are to thee in all thy border, and oil thou dost not pour out, for thine olive doth fall off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Sons and daughters thou dost beget, and they are not with thee, for they go into captivity;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all thy trees and the fruit of thy ground doth the locust possess;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the sojourner who [is] in thy midst goeth up above thee very high, and thou goest down very low;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he doth lend [to] thee, and thou dost not lend [to] him; he is for head, and thou art for tail.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And come upon thee have all these curses, and they have pursued thee, and overtaken thee, till thou art destroyed, because thou hast not hearkened to the voice of Jehovah thy God, to keep His commands, and His statutes, which he hath commanded thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have been on thee for a sign and for a wonder, also on thy seed -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because that thou hast not served Jehovah thy God with joy, and with gladness of heart, because of the abundance of all things --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou hast served thine enemies, whom Jehovah sendeth against thee, in hunger, and in thirst, and in nakedness, and in lack of all things; and he hath put a yoke of iron on thy neck, till He hath destroyed thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah doth lift up against thee a nation, from afar, from the end of the earth, as the eagle it flieth; a nation whose tongue thou hast not heard,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a nation -- fierce of countenance -- which accepteth not the face of the aged, and the young doth not favour;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath eaten the fruit of thy cattle, and the fruit of thy ground, till thou art destroyed; which leaveth not to thee corn, new wine, and oil, increase of thine oxen, and wealth of thy flock, till it hath destroyed thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath laid siege to thee in all thy gates, till thy walls come down, the high and the fenced ones in which thou art trusting, in all thy land; yea, it hath laid siege to thee in all thy gates, in all thy land, which Jehovah thy God hath given to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast eaten the fruit of thy body, flesh of thy sons and thy daughters (whom Jehovah thy God hath given to thee), in the siege, and in the straitness with which thine enemies do straiten thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The man who is tender in thee, and who [is] very delicate -- his eye is evil against his brother, and against the wife of his bosom, and against the remnant of his sons whom he leaveth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: against giving to one of them of the flesh of his sons whom he eateth, because he hath nothing left to him, in the siege, and in the straitness with which thine enemy doth straiten thee in all thy gates.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The tender woman in thee, and the delicate, who hath not tried the sole of her foot to place on the ground because of delicateness and because of tenderness -- her eye is evil against the husband of her bosom, and against her son, and against her daughter,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and against her seed which cometh out from between her feet, even against her sons whom she doth bear, for she doth eat them for the lacking of all things in secret, in the siege and in the straitness with which thine enemy doth straiten thee within thy gates.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If thou dost not observe to do all the words of this law which are written in this book, to fear this honoured and fearful name -- Jehovah thy God --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then hath Jehovah made wonderful thy strokes, and the strokes of thy seed -- great strokes, and stedfast, and evil sicknesses, and stedfast.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And He hath brought back on thee all the diseases of Egypt, of the presence of which thou hast been afraid, and they have cleaved to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: also every sickness and every stroke which is not written in the book of this law; Jehovah doth cause them to go up upon thee till thou art destroyed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have been left with few men, instead of which ye have been as stars of the heavens for multitude, because thou hast not hearkened to the voice of Jehovah thy God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, as Jehovah hath rejoiced over you to do you good, and to multiply you, so doth Jehovah rejoice over you to destroy you, and to lay you waste; and ye have been pulled away from off the ground whither thou art going in to possess it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath scattered thee among all the peoples, from the end of the earth even unto the end of the earth; and thou hast served there other gods which thou hast not known, thou and thy fathers -- wood and stone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And among those nations thou dost not rest, yea, there is no resting-place for the sole of thy foot, and Jehovah hath given to thee there a trembling heart, and failing of eyes, and grief of soul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy life hath been hanging in suspense before thee, and thou hast been afraid by night and by day, and dost not believe in thy life;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the morning thou sayest, O that it were evening! and in the evening thou sayest, O that it were morning! from the fear of thy heart, with which thou art afraid, and from the sight of thine eyes which thou seest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah hath brought thee back to Egypt with ships, by a way of which I said to thee, Thou dost not add any more to see it, and ye have sold yourselves there to thine enemies, for men-servants and for maid-servants, and there is no buyer.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] the words of the covenant which Jehovah hath commanded Moses to make with the sons of Israel in the land of Moab, apart from the covenant which He made with them in Horeb.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses calleth unto all Israel, and saith unto them, 'Ye -- ye have seen all that which Jehovah hath done before your eyes in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh, and to all his servants, and to all his land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the great trials which thine eyes have seen, the signs, and those great wonders;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath not given to you a heart to know, and eyes to see, and ears to hear, till this day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I cause you to go forty years in a wilderness; your garments have not been consumed from off you, and thy shoe hath not worn away from off thy foot;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: bread ye have not eaten, and wine and strong drink ye have not drunk, so that ye know that I [am] Jehovah your God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye come in unto this place, and Sihon king of Heshbon -- also Og king of Bashan -- doth come out to meet us, to battle, and we smite them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and take their land, and give it for an inheritance to the Reubenite, and to the Gadite, and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have kept the words of this covenant, and done them, so that ye cause all that ye do to prosper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye are standing to-day, all of you, before Jehovah your God -- your heads, your tribes, your elders, and your authorities -- every man of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: your infants, your wives, and thy sojourner who [is] in the midst of thy camps, from the hewer of thy wood unto the drawer of thy water --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thy passing over into the covenant of Jehovah thy God, and into His oath which Jehovah thy God is making with thee to-day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in order to establish thee to-day to Him for a people, and He Himself is thy God, as He hath spoken to thee, and as He hath sworn to thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And not with you alone am I making this covenant and this oath;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but with him who is here with us, standing to-day before Jehovah our God, and with him who is not here with us to-day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for ye have known how ye dwelt in the land of Egypt, and how we passed by through the midst of the nations which ye have passed by;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye see their abominations, and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which [are] with them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest there be among you a man or woman, or family or tribe, whose heart is turning to-day from Jehovah our God, to go to serve the gods of those nations, lest there be in you a root fruitful of gall and wormwood:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, in his hearing the words of this oath, and he hath blessed himself in his heart, saying, I have peace, though in the stubbornness of my heart I go on, in order to end the fulness with the thirst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah is not willing to be propitious to him, for then doth the anger of Jehovah smoke, also His zeal, against that man, and lain down on him hath all the oath which is written in this book, and Jehovah hath blotted out his name from under the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath separated him for evil, out of all the tribes of Israel, according to all the oaths of the covenant which is written in this book of the law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the latter generation of your sons who rise after you, and the stranger who cometh in from a land afar off, have said when they have seen the strokes of that land, and its sicknesses which Jehovah hath sent into it, --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ([with] brimstone and salt is the whole land burnt, it is not sown, nor doth it shoot up, nor doth there go up on it any herb, like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboim, which Jehovah overturned in His anger, and in His fury,) --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yea, all the nations have said, Wherefore hath Jehovah done thus to this land? what the heat of this great anger?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And they have said, Because that they have forsaken the covenant of Jehovah, God of their fathers, which He made with them in His bringing them out of the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they go and serve other gods, and bow themselves to them -- gods which they have not known, and which He hath not apportioned to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anger of Jehovah burneth against that land, to bring in on it all the reviling that is written in this book,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah doth pluck them from off their ground in anger, and in fury, and in great wrath, and doth cast them unto another land, as [at] this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The things hidden [are] to Jehovah our God, and the things revealed [are] to us and to our sons -- to the age, to do all the words of this law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when all these things come upon thee, the blessing and the reviling, which I have set before thee, and thou hast brought [them] back unto thy heart, among all the nations whither Jehovah thy God hath driven thee away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast turned back unto Jehovah thy God, and hearkened to His voice, according to all that I am commanding thee to-day, thou and thy sons, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then hath Jehovah thy God turned back [to] thy captivity, and pitied thee, yea, He hath turned back and gathered thee out of all the peoples whither Jehovah thy God hath scattered thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If thine outcast is in the extremity of the heavens, thence doth Jehovah thy God gather thee, and thence He doth take thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath brought thee in unto the land which thy fathers have possessed, and thou hast inherited it, and He hath done thee good, and multiplied thee above thy fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Jehovah thy God hath circumcised thy heart, and the heart of thy seed, to love Jehovah thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, for the sake of thy life;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath put all this oath on thine enemies, and on those hating thee, who have pursued thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou dost turn back, and hast hearkened to the voice of Jehovah, and hast done all His commands which I am commanding thee to-day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah thy God hath made thee abundant in every work of thy hand, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle, and in the fruit of thy ground, for good; for Jehovah turneth back to rejoice over thee for good, as He rejoiced over thy fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thou dost hearken to the voice of Jehovah thy God, to keep His commands, and His statutes, which are written in the book of this law, for thou turnest back unto Jehovah thy God, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For this command which I am commanding thee to-day, it is not too wonderful for thee, nor [is] it far off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It is not in the heavens, -- saying, Who doth go up for us into the heavens, and doth take it for us, and doth cause us to hear it -- that we may do it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it [is] not beyond the sea, -- saying, Who doth pass over for us beyond the sea, and doth take it for us, and doth cause us to hear it -- that we may do it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For very near unto thee is the word, in thy mouth, and in thy heart -- to do it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'See, I have set before thee to-day life and good, and death and evil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in that I am commanding thee to-day to love Jehovah thy God, to walk in His ways, and to keep His commands, and His statutes, and His judgments; and thou hast lived and multiplied, and Jehovah thy God hath blessed thee in the land whither thou art going in to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if thy heart doth turn, and thou dost not hearken, and hast been driven away, and hast bowed thyself to other gods, and served them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have declared to you this day, that ye do certainly perish, ye do not prolong days on the ground which thou art passing over the Jordan to go in thither to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I have caused to testify against you to-day the heavens and the earth; life and death I have set before thee, the blessing and the reviling; and thou hast fixed on life, so that thou dost live, thou and thy seed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to love Jehovah thy God, to hearken to His voice, and to cleave to Him (for He [is] thy life, and the length of thy days), to dwell on the ground which Jehovah hath sworn to thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses goeth and speaketh these words unto all Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith unto them, 'A son of a hundred and twenty years [am] I to-day; I am not able any more to go out and to come in, and Jehovah hath said unto me, Thou dost not pass over this Jordan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah thy God He is passing over before thee, He doth destroy these nations from before thee, and thou hast possessed them; Joshua -- he is passing over before thee as Jehovah hath spoken,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath done to them as he hath done to Sihon and to Og, kings of the Amorite, and to their land, whom He destroyed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah hath given them before your face, and ye have done to them according to all the command which I have commanded you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be strong and courageous, fear not, nor be terrified because of them, for Jehovah thy God [is] He who is going with thee; He doth not fail thee nor forsake thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses calleth for Joshua, and saith unto him before the eyes of all Israel, 'Be strong and courageous, for thou -- thou dost go in with this people unto the land which Jehovah hath sworn to their fathers to give to them, and thou -- thou dost cause them to inherit it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah [is] He who is going before thee, He himself is with thee; He doth not fail thee nor forsake thee; fear not, nor be affrighted.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses writeth this law, and giveth it unto the priests (sons of Levi, those bearing the ark of the covenant of Jehovah), and unto all the elders of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses commandeth them, saying, 'At the end of seven years, in the appointed time, the year of release, in the feast of booths,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the coming in of all Israel to see the face of Jehovah in the place which He chooseth, thou dost proclaim this law before all Israel, in their ears.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Assemble the people, the men, and the women, and the infants, and thy sojourner who [is] within thy gates, so that they hear, and so that they learn, and have feared Jehovah your God, and observed to do all the words of this law;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their sons, who have not known, do hear, and have learned to fear Jehovah your God all the days which ye are living on the ground whither ye are passing over the Jordan to possess it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Lo, thy days have drawn near to die; call Joshua, and station yourselves in the tent of meeting, and I charge him;' and Moses goeth -- Joshua also -- and they station themselves in the tent of meeting,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah is seen in the tent, in a pillar of a cloud; and the pillar of the cloud standeth at the opening of the tent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Lo, thou art lying down with thy fathers, and this people hath risen, and gone a-whoring after the gods of the stranger of the land into the midst of which it hath entered, and forsaken Me, and broken My covenant which I made with it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Mine anger hath burned against it in that day, and I have forsaken them, and hidden My face from them, and it hath been for consumption, and many evils and distresses have found it, and it hath said in that day, Is it not because that my God is not in my midst -- these evils have found me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I certainly hide My face in that day for all the evil which it hath done, for it hath turned unto other gods.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, write for you this song, and teach it the sons of Israel; put it in their mouths, so that this song is to Me for a witness against the sons of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I bring them in unto the ground which I have sworn to their fathers -- flowing with milk and honey, and they have eaten, and been satisfied, and been fat, and have turned unto other gods, and they have served them, and despised Me, and broken My covenant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been, when many evils and distresses do meet it, that this song hath testified to its face for a witness; for it is not forgotten out of the mouth of its seed, for I have known its imagining which it is doing to-day, before I bring them in unto the land of which I have sworn.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses writeth this song on that day, and doth teach it the sons of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He commandeth Joshua son of Nun, and saith, 'Be strong and courageous, for thou dost bring in the sons of Israel unto the land which I have sworn to them, and I -- I am with thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when Moses finisheth to write the words of this law on a book till their completion,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that Moses commandeth the Levites bearing the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take this Book of the Law, and thou hast set it on the side of the ark of the covenant of Jehovah your God, and it hath been there against thee for a witness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I -- I have known thy rebellion, and thy stiff neck; lo, in my being yet alive with you to-day, rebellious ye have been with Jehovah, and also surely after my death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Assemble unto me all the elders of your tribes, and your authorities, and I speak in their ears these words, and cause to testify against them the heavens and the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I have known that after my death ye do very corruptly, and have turned aside out of the way which I commanded you, and evil hath met you in the latter end of the days, because ye do the evil thing in the eyes of Jehovah, to make Him angry with the work of your hands.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh in the ears of all the assembly of Israel the words of this song, till their completion: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Give ear, O heavens, and I speak; And thou dost hear, O earth, sayings of my mouth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Drop as rain doth My doctrine; Flow as dew doth My sayings; As storms on the tender grass, And as showers on the herb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the Name of Jehovah I proclaim, Ascribe ye greatness to our God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Rock! -- perfect [is] His work, For all His ways [are] just; God of stedfastness, and without iniquity: Righteous and upright [is] He.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It hath done corruptly to Him; Their blemish is not His sons', A generation perverse and crooked!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To Jehovah do ye act thus, O people foolish and not wise? Is not He thy father -- thy possessor? He made thee, and doth establish thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember days of old -- Understand the years of many generations -- Ask thy father, and he doth tell thee; Thine elders, and they say to thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the Most High causing nations to inherit, In His separating sons of Adam -- He setteth up the borders of the peoples By the number of the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah's portion [is] His people, Jacob [is] the line of His inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He findeth him in a land -- a desert, And in a void -- a howling wilderness, He turneth him round -- He causeth him to understand -- He keepeth him as the apple of His eye.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As an eagle waketh up its nest, Over its young ones fluttereth, Spreadeth its wings -- taketh them, Beareth them on its pinions; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah alone doth lead him, And there is no strange god with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He maketh him ride on high places of earth, And he eateth increase of the fields, And He maketh him suck honey from a rock, And oil out of the flint of a rock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Butter of the herd, and milk of the flock, With fat of lambs, and rams, sons of Bashan, And he-goats, with fat of kidneys of wheat; And of the blood of the grape thou dost drink wine!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jeshurun waxeth fat, and doth kick: Thou hast been fat -- thou hast been thick, Thou hast been covered. And he leaveth God who made him, And dishonoureth the Rock of his salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They make Him zealous with strangers, With abominations they make Him angry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They sacrifice to demons -- no god! Gods they have not known -- New ones -- from the vicinity they came; Not feared them have your fathers!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Rock that begat thee thou forgettest, And neglectest God who formeth thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah seeth and despiseth -- For the provocation of His sons and His daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith: I hide My face from them, I see what [is] their latter end; For a froward generation [are] they, Sons in whom is no stedfastness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have made Me zealous by 'no-god,' They made Me angry by their vanities; And I make them zealous by 'no-people,' By a foolish nation I make them angry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a fire hath been kindled in Mine anger, And it burneth unto Sheol -- the lowest, And consumeth earth and its increase, And setteth on fire foundations of mountains.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I gather upon them evils, Mine arrows I consume upon them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Exhausted by famine, And consumed by heat, and bitter destruction. And the teeth of beasts I send upon them, With poison of fearful things of the dust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Without bereave doth the sword, And at the inner-chambers -- fear, Both youth and virgin, Suckling with man of grey hair.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have said: I blow them away, I cause their remembrance to cease from man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If not -- the anger of an enemy I fear, Lest their adversaries know -- Lest they say, Our hand is high, And Jehovah hath not wrought all this.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a nation lost to counsels [are] they, And there is no understanding in them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If they were wise -- They deal wisely [with] this; They attend to their latter end:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How doth one pursue a thousand, And two cause a myriad to flee! If not -- that their rock hath sold them, And Jehovah hath shut them up?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For not as our Rock [is] their rock, (And our enemies [are] judges!)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For of the vine of Sodom their vine [is], And of the fields of Gomorrah; Their grapes [are] grapes of gall -- They have bitter clusters;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The poison of dragons [is] their wine And the fierce venom of asps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it not laid up with Me? Sealed among My treasures?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine [are] vengeance and recompense, At the due time -- doth their foot slide; For near is a day of their calamity, And haste do things prepared for them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah doth judge His people, And for His servants doth repent Himself. For He seeth -- the going away of power, And none is restrained and left.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He hath said, Where [are] their gods -- The rock in which they trusted;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Which the fat of their sacrifices do eat, They drink the wine of their libation! Let them arise and help you, Let it be for you a hiding-place!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See ye, now, that I -- I [am] He, And there is no god with Me: I put to death, and I keep alive; I have smitten, and I heal; And there is not from My hand a deliverer,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I lift up unto the heavens My hand, And have said, I live -- to the age!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have sharpened the brightness of My sword, And My hand doth lay hold on judgment, I turn back vengeance to Mine adversaries, And to those hating Me -- I repay!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I make drunk Mine arrows with blood, And My sword devoureth flesh, From the blood of the pierced and captive, From the head of the freemen of the enemy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing ye nations -- [with] his people, For the blood of His servants He avengeth, And vengeance He turneth back on His adversaries, And hath pardoned His land -- His people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses cometh and speaketh all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he and Hoshea son of Nun;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses finisheth to speak all these words unto all Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith unto them, 'Set your heart to all the words which I am testifying against you to-day, that ye command your sons to observe to do all the words of this law,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it [is] not a vain thing for you, for it [is] your life, and by this thing ye prolong days on the ground whither ye are passing over the Jordan to possess it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, in this self-same day, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Go up unto this mount Abarim, mount Nebo, which [is] in the land of Moab, which [is] on the front of Jericho, and see the land of Canaan which I am giving to the sons of Israel for a possession;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and die in the mount whither thou art going up, and be gathered unto thy people, as Aaron thy brother hath died in the mount Hor, and is gathered unto his people:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because ye trespassed against me in the midst of the sons of Israel at the waters of Meribath-Kadesh, the wilderness of Zin -- because ye sanctified Me not in the midst of the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but over-against thou seest the land, and thither thou dost not go in, unto the land which I am giving to the sons of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this [is] the blessing [with] which Moses the man of God blessed the sons of Israel before his death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith: -- 'Jehovah from Sinai hath come, And hath risen from Seir for them; He hath shone from mount Paran, And hath come [with] myriads of holy ones; At His right hand [are] springs for them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also He [is] loving the peoples; All His holy ones [are] in thy hand, And they -- they sat down at thy foot, [Each] He lifteth up at thy words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A law hath Moses commanded us, A possession of the assembly of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he is in Jeshurun king, In the heads of the people gathering together, The tribes of Israel!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let Reuben live, and not die, And let his men be a number.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this [is] for Judah; and he saith: -- Hear, O Jehovah, the voice of Judah, And unto his people do Thou bring him in; His hand hath striven for him, And an help from his adversaries art Thou.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Levi he said: -- Thy Thummim and thy Urim [are] for thy pious one, Whom Thou hast tried in Massah, Thou dost strive with Him at the waters of Meribah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is saying of his father and his mother, I have not seen him; And his brethren he hath not discerned, And his sons he hath not known; For they have observed Thy saying, And Thy covenant they keep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They teach Thy judgments to Jacob, And Thy law to Israel; They put perfume in Thy nose, And whole burnt-offering on Thine altar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bless, O Jehovah, his strength, And the work of his hands Thou acceptest, Smite the loins of his withstanders, And of those hating him -- that they rise not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of Benjamin he said: -- The beloved of Jehovah doth tabernacle confidently by him, Covering him over all the day; Yea, between his shoulders He doth tabernacle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Joseph he said: -- Blessed of Jehovah [is] his land, By precious things of the heavens, By dew, and by the deep crouching beneath,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And by precious things -- fruits of the sun, And by precious things -- cast forth by the moons,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And by chief things -- of the ancient mountains, And by precious things -- of the age-during heights,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And by precious things -- of earth and its fulness, And the good pleasure Of Him who is dwelling in the bush, -- Let it come for the head of Joseph, And for the crown of him Who is separate from his brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His honour [is] a firstling of his ox, And his horns [are] horns of a reem; By them peoples he doth push together To the ends of earth; And they [are] the myriads of Ephraim, And they [are] the thousands of Manasseh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Zebulun he said: -- Rejoice, O Zebulun, in thy going out, And, O Issachar, in thy tents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Peoples [to] the mountain they call, There they sacrifice righteous sacrifices; For the abundance of the seas they suck, And hidden things hidden in the sand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Gad he said: -- Blessed of the Enlarger [is] Gad, As a lioness he doth tabernacle, And hath torn the arm -- also the crown!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he provideth the first part for himself, For there the portion of the lawgiver is covered, And he cometh [with] the heads of the people; The righteousness of Jehovah he hath done, And His judgments with Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Dan he said: -- Dan [is] a lion's whelp; he doth leap from Bashan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Naphtali he said: -- O Naphtali, satisfied with pleasure, And full of the blessing of Jehovah, West and south possess thou.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Asher he said: -- Blessed with sons [is] Asher, Let him be accepted by his brethren, And dipping in oil his foot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Iron and brass [are] thy shoes, And as thy days -- thy strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is none like the God of Jeshurun, Riding the heavens in thy help, And in His excellency the skies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A habitation [is] the eternal God, And beneath [are] arms age-during. And He casteth out from thy presence the enemy, and saith, 'Destroy!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel doth tabernacle [in] confidence alone; The eye of Jacob [is] unto a land of corn and wine; Also His heavens drop down dew.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O thy happiness, O Israel! who is like thee? A people saved by Jehovah, The shield of thy help, And He who [is] the sword of thine excellency: And thine enemies are subdued for thee, And thou on their high places dost tread.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses goeth up from the plains of Moab unto mount Nebo, the top of Pisgah, which [is] on the front of Jericho, and Jehovah sheweth him all the land -- Gilead unto Dan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim, and Manasseh, and all the land of Judah unto the further sea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the south, and the circuit of the valley of Jericho, the city of palms, unto Zoar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto him, 'This [is] the land which I have sworn to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, To thy seed I give it; I have caused thee to see with thine eyes, and thither thou dost not pass over.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses, servant of the Lord, dieth there, in the land of Moab, according to the command of Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He burieth him in a valley in the land of Moab, over-against Beth-Peor, and no man hath known his burying place unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses [is] a son of a hundred and twenty years when he dieth; his eye hath not become dim, nor hath his moisture fled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel bewail Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days; and the days of weeping [and] mourning for Moses are completed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joshua son of Nun is full of the spirit of wisdom, for Moses had laid his hands upon him, and the sons of Israel hearken unto him, and do as Jehovah commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there hath not arisen a prophet any more in Israel like Moses, whom Jehovah hath known face unto face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in reference to all the signs and the wonders which Jehovah sent him to do in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh, and to all his servants, and to all his land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in reference to all the strong hand, and to all the great fear which Moses did before the eyes of all Israel.
